target.title: fix(memory-search): stop hybrid fusion from discounting vector-only multimodal results
target.body:
## Summary

Hybrid memory search ranks results with `finalScore = vectorWeight * vectorScore + textWeight * textScore`. `mergeHybridResults` (`extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.ts`) applied that formula uniformly to every candidate:

```ts
const score = params.vectorWeight * entry.vectorScore + params.textWeight * entry.textScore;
```

Image and audio chunks are different from text chunks in one structural way: their only text is the synthetic label `buildMemoryMultimodalLabel` produces, e.g. `Image file: media/IMG_5821.png` (`packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/multimodal.ts`). A natural-language query shares no words with that label, so a media chunk's BM25/text score is near zero and it is seeded into the merge with `textScore: 0`. Under the default `0.7` vector / `0.3` text weights (`src/agents/memory-search.ts`), a semantically strong media match is scaled to 70% of its vector score, so it ranks below a weaker text chunk that happens to match query keywords. With multimodal memory enabled, relevant stored image/audio context is suppressed from search and therefore omitted from agent context.

The fix makes fusion modality-aware. Instead of a special-case `score = vectorScore` branch for media (which would put media and text on inconsistent scales), `mergeHybridResults` renormalizes the configured weights over the signals that are meaningful for each candidate:

```diff
-    const score = params.vectorWeight * entry.vectorScore + params.textWeight * entry.textScore;
+    const effectiveTextWeight =
+      params.isNonTextMediaPath?.(entry.path) === true ? 0 : params.textWeight;
+    const weightSum = params.vectorWeight + effectiveTextWeight;
+    const weighted = params.vectorWeight * entry.vectorScore + effectiveTextWeight * entry.textScore;
+    const score = weightSum > 0 ? weighted / weightSum : 0;
```

For a non-text media chunk the text weight drops to `0` and the score collapses to its vector score, on the same `[0,1]` scale as text candidates. For text candidates nothing changes: the resolved weights already sum to 1, so dividing by `weightSum` is a no-op and text-only ordering is preserved.

The manager supplies the predicate from the already-known file path, reusing the same classifier the sync path uses (`extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts`):

```ts
isNonTextMediaPath: (path) =>
  classifyMemoryMultimodalPath(path, this.settings.multimodal) !== null,
```

`classifyMemoryMultimodalPath` returns `null` whenever multimodal search is disabled, so the predicate is `false` for every path and behavior is byte-for-byte identical to today on text-only setups. Modality is derived, not persisted, so there is no schema change or migration.

What is intentionally out of scope: persisting a modality column on chunks, and any change to the text-only ranking path.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: hybrid fusion discounted vector-only image/audio matches by the configured text weight, so a semantically strong media result ranked below a weaker keyword-matching text result.
Real environment tested: the real shipped `mergeHybridResults` fusion function (the exact code this PR changes), driven with production-representative retrieval scores for the query `satellite view of coastal erosion`, with MMR and temporal decay disabled to isolate the fusion math. Inputs: an indexed image that is the strongest semantic match (`vectorScore 0.86`) whose only text is the `Image file: <path>` label, so it is absent from the keyword set (`textScore 0`); and a memory note that is a slightly weaker semantic match (`vectorScore 0.78`) but shares query words and earns a strong BM25 score (`textScore 0.95`).
Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the fusion with the patch applied, then reverted only `hybrid.ts` to `main` (`git stash`) and re-ran the identical inputs for the before case.
Evidence after fix:
```
BEFORE FIX (main):
  #1  score=0.8310  vec=0.78  text=0.95  memory/coast-notes.md
  #2  score=0.6020  vec=0.86  text=0.00  media/IMG_5821.png    <- strongest semantic match, buried below weaker text

AFTER FIX:
  #1  score=0.8600  vec=0.86  text=0.00  media/IMG_5821.png    <- now surfaces
  #2  score=0.8310  vec=0.78  text=0.95  memory/coast-notes.md  <- text score unchanged
```
Observed result after fix: the image is renormalized to its vector score (0.86) and ranks first; the text note's score is identical before and after (0.8310), confirming text-only ordering is untouched. On `main` the same image is discounted to 0.602 and loses to the weaker note.
What was not tested: a live Gemini-keyed multimodal SQLite index end-to-end.
Proof limitations or environment constraints: no Gemini API key is available locally, and the in-repo test embedding harness ties the literal word `image` to both the vector dimension and the synthetic label, so it cannot isolate the semantic-vs-keyword case end-to-end through `MemoryIndexManager.search`. The behavior this PR changes lives entirely in the fusion function, which was driven directly with representative scores. `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts` already confirms multimodal files index and are retrievable.

## Verification

- New before/after matrix above run against the real `mergeHybridResults` before and after the fix.
- Added regression coverage in `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.test.ts`: one test for the semantic-query media case (vector-only media scored on its vector signal without the text-weight discount, ranking above an equally-vector-relevant text chunk that lacks a keyword match), and one guard that text-candidate scoring is unchanged when a media predicate is supplied. The existing suite did not exercise this because the prior multimodal test queries the literal words `image` and `audio`, which match the synthetic label.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/hybrid.test.ts` (7 pass).
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-search.test.ts` (20 pass).
- `tsgo` extensions typecheck passes; oxlint clean on the three changed files.
- The throwaway proof script used for the before/after capture is not committed; the diff is the production fix plus the regression tests.

Closes #44540

target.title: fix(agents): retry thinking-only errored turns
target.body:
Summary
- Fixes #91953.
- Retries replay-safe `stopReason="error"` turns when the assistant emitted only non-visible thinking content before the provider error.
- Keeps the existing safety boundary: no retry after potential side effects, no retry when visible assistant text exists, and no retry for empty error turns with non-zero output but no thinking-only content.

Verification
- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.empty-error-retry.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts`

Real behavior proof
- behavior: A replay-safe assistant turn that ends with `stopReason="error"` after emitting only thinking content is retried by the embedded runner instead of surfacing a silent incomplete-turn error.
- environment: Local source checkout at `9a6c71a47d` plus this branch, Node 22.22.2, pnpm 11.2.2, Vitest agents project.
- steps: Added a regression that returns an Anthropic-style assistant message with `stopReason="error"`, a signed thinking block, and `usage.output=1120`, followed by a successful second attempt.

- observedResult: The runner now retries the non-visible errored thinking-only turn and completes from the successful second attempt, while side-effect and visible-progress guards remain covered.
- notTested: I did not replay the original live Vertex AI / Claude Opus provider failure. The proof uses the embedded-runner harness to reproduce the exact terminal assistant shape from the issue and verify the shared retry path.

target.title: fix(logging): prune non-idle stale diagnostic session states (fixes #91697)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: `pruneDiagnosticSessionStates()` only cleans up diagnostic entries where `state === "idle"` AND `queueDepth <= 0`. Entries that never transition back to "idle" — for example after a failed stuck-session recovery — accumulate in the process-local Map indefinitely, holding activity snapshots, tool call history, and queue metadata in V8 heap until the 2000-entry FIFO cap is reached.
- **Root Cause**: When `requestStuckSessionRecovery()` fails (e.g. stale generation check, already-closed session), `recoveryOutcomeMutatesSessionState()` returns `false` for `status: "failed"` outcomes, so `applyRecoveryOutcomeToDiagnosticState()` never sets the diagnostic state back to "idle". The prune guard `isIdle && queueDepth <= 0 && ageMs > TTL` then skips these orphaned non-idle entries forever.
- **Fix**: Add a fallback deletion branch in `pruneDiagnosticSessionStates()` that removes any non-idle entry older than `SESSION_STATE_TTL_MS` (30 min), regardless of state. The existing idle+empty-queue path is unchanged; the new `else if (!isIdle)` branch catches ghost entries that survived failed recovery.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/logging/diagnostic-session-state.ts` — added early `continue` for entries within TTL, then `else if (!isIdle)` fallback delete in the prune loop
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - `recoveryOutcomeMutatesSessionState()` — not changed; recovery coordinator behavior is left alone to avoid side effects
  - The `SESSION_STATE_MAX_ENTRIES` FIFO cap — remains as a hard upper bound (2000 entries)

### Reproduction

1. Gateway with `diagnostics.stuckSessionAbortMs` configured (e.g. 300000)
2. A session stalls → `stuckSessionAbortMs` fires → recovery attempts
3. Recovery fails (e.g. stale generation check `isDiagnosticSessionStateCurrent` returns false)
4. Outcome `{ status: "failed", action: "none" }` skips the idle transition
5. **Before this PR**: entry stays in "processing" state in the Map forever (up to the 2000-entry cap), never pruned by the 30-min TTL sweep
6. **After this PR**: entry is removed once its `lastActivity` age exceeds `SESSION_STATE_TTL_MS` (30 min)

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed (91697)**: The diagnostic session state Map accumulates non-idle ghost entries indefinitely after failed stuck-session recoveries. After the fix, any non-idle entry older than the 30-minute TTL is cleaned up on the next prune sweep, preventing unbounded heap growth from orphaned diagnostic state.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22 — Vitest with fake timers against `pruneDiagnosticSessionStates` and `getDiagnosticSessionState`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668001395/openclawProject1

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  69 passed (69)
   Start at  22:18:16
   Duration  2.40s (transform 982ms, setup 243ms, import 1.77s, tests 209ms, environment 0ms)
```

**Observed result after fix**: All 69 diagnostic tests pass, including the new "evicts stale non-idle session states (ghost entries after failed recovery)" test. The test creates a non-idle ("processing") entry with a 31-minute-old `lastActivity` timestamp and a non-zero `queueDepth`, then triggers a prune via `getDiagnosticSessionState`. After the prune sweep, the ghost entry is deleted and only the fresh entry remains. The existing "evicts stale idle session states" test continues to pass unchanged, confirming the idle-path behavior is preserved.

**What was not tested**: Live Gateway integration was not driven — the test uses Vitest fake timers to simulate the TTL window and does not exercise the full stuck-session recovery→failed→prune pipeline end-to-end with a real Gateway process.

**Repro confirmation**: The new test case fails on origin/main before the patch — non-idle stale entries survive the prune sweep — and passes after the fix, confirming the ghost entry is cleaned up once it exceeds the TTL regardless of state.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: A genuinely long-running session that stays "processing" for >30 minutes could be incorrectly pruned.
  **Mitigation**: The `lastActivity` field is updated on every diagnostic heartbeat (not wall-clock time since state transition). A genuinely active processing session refreshes `lastActivity` continually, so `ageMs` stays well under 30 minutes. Only entries with no activity for >30 minutes are evicted.
- **Risk**: The fallback branch could mask a recovery coordinator bug by silently cleaning up entries that recovery should have handled.
  **Mitigation**: The prune sweep is a garbage-collection concern, not an error-handling concern. Recovery coordinator bugs should be fixed separately; this change only ensures memory doesn't grow unbounded when they occur.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] agents (diagnostic session state tracking)
- [x] logging

### Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts` — covers prune behavior for idle and non-idle stale entries
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic-stability-bundle.test.ts` — covers downstream diagnostic stability snapshot consumers

### Review Findings Addressed

N/A (initial submission)

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #91697

target.title: fix(tools): propagate external content provenance to policy hooks
target.body:
Summary:
- Adds structured external-content provenance to tool-call policy hook types.
- Propagates provenance through HookContext, before_tool_call plugin events, trusted tool policy events, and tool hook context.
- Detects EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT markers from the active embedded run prompt/session context.
- Refreshes provenance after same-run tool results, so fetch/browser/file external content is visible to later policy hooks in the same run when the wrapped tools share the same hook context.
- Documents the public hook field and source semantics.
- Refreshes the plugin SDK type barrel so extension package-boundary cache keys invalidate when hook contract shapes change.

Why:
- External content is already wrapped as untrusted prompt text.
- Before this change, the post-model tool-call policy boundary did not receive structured provenance showing whether external content was present.
- Policy plugins could see the tool name, params, tool kind/input kind, run/toolCallId, derived paths, and session context, but not whether the tool call followed browser, web_fetch, email, webhook, file-fetch, or other untrusted external context.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Same-run external content provenance reaches the later before_tool_call policy boundary after a web_fetch result introduces wrapped EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT. The checked behavior is web_fetch first, then exec later in the same shared HookContext.

Real environment tested:
Local OpenClaw checkout on branch fix/tool-call-external-provenance at PR head e8800a11833801434e6d48a4a0b3f9b03d06d958, running on Linux with Node v24.15.0 and pnpm 11.2.2. The proof used the real active OpenClaw plugin registry, a real trusted tool policy entry, and the real wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook runtime path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
Ran a live `node --import tsx --eval ...` harness in the PR worktree. The harness registered a trusted tool policy in the active plugin registry, wrapped a web_fetch tool and a bash/exec tool with wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook, executed web_fetch first, then executed exec in the same shared hook context.

Evidence after fix:
Terminal capture / copied live output from the real local OpenClaw proof run:

```text
[proof] pr=91800
[proof] head_sha=e8800a11833801434e6d48a4a0b3f9b03d06d958
[proof] using real wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook runtime path
[proof] trusted policy count=1
[proof] shared HookContext initially has externalContent=null
[proof-policy] tool=web_fetch toolCallId=proof-web-fetch-call event.externalContent=null ctx.externalContent=null
[proof-tool] web_fetch returning wrapped external content
[proof-policy] tool=exec toolCallId=proof-exec-call event.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]} ctx.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]}
[proof-tool] exec would run redacted command
[proof-result] first_call=web_fetch externalContent=null status=PASS
[proof-result] second_call=exec event.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]} ctx.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]} status=PASS
[proof-result] PASS: same-run external web_fetch content reached later before_tool_call policy boundary through wrapped tool execution
```

Observed result after fix:
Before the web_fetch result returned, the policy hook saw no externalContent. After web_fetch returned wrapped external content with Source: Web Fetch, the later exec policy hook received event.externalContent and ctx.externalContent as {"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]}. This proves the same-run provenance signal reaches the later policy boundary after external content is introduced.

What was not tested:
This proof does not claim full browser automation coverage or production split-context run-owned state coverage. It specifically proves the same-run web_fetch-to-exec policy signal path exercised by this PR.

## Validation

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts
- pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile
- git diff --check

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: `tools.toolSearch.mode: "directory"` keeps hidden catalog tool schemas deferred while still letting the agent loop hydrate and execute a requested catalog tool through the directory-mode tool path.
- Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox using hosted Testbox live credentials from `~/.openclaw-testbox-live.profile`; no local keys were used. Crabbox `0.27.0`, provider `blacksmith-testbox`, Testbox `tbx_01ktppx0dxxps5rf2qtzhg4jwy`, feature-proof head `35198cf3`, merge-refresh head `796472c699`, model `openai/gpt-5.5`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --label pr-91632-live-plugin-tool-directory-ci --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- <hosted live plugin-tool directory-mode runner>`. The remote runner used the hosted live profile, injected `tools.toolSearch: { enabled: true, mode: "directory" }` into the live plugin-tool E2E fixture config, then ran `CI=1 pnpm test:docker:live-plugin-tool`.
- Evidence after fix:

- Observed result after fix: The hosted E2E built and packed OpenClaw, installed a packed fixture plugin with a real npm dependency, enabled the plugin, ran a live `openai/gpt-5.5` agent turn in directory tool-search mode, and verified the model called the plugin tool successfully. After merge-refresh commit `796472c699`, focused local proof rechecked the directory-mode unit/config/agent-loop path plus the two previously failing current-main agent tests, and autoreview returned clean.
- What was not tested: Direct Azure Crabbox was not run because this laptop is not logged into Azure Crabbox. Exact-head hosted live E2E was not rerun after the merge-only refresh from `9d2c4b5` to `796472c699`; the hosted live behavior proof remains from feature head `35198cf3`, and the latest change only merged current `upstream/main` plus reran focused local proof/autoreview. An earlier hosted Testbox attempt `tbx_01ktppshrctts4n4agcg7d3jpb` failed before E2E on pnpm's non-TTY dependency reconciliation prompt; the rerun set `CI=1` and passed.

target.title: Harden memory wiki bridge imports
target.body:
## Summary

- Add bounded retry handling for transient `FsSafeError: path-mismatch` while writing imported memory-wiki source pages.
- Coalesce concurrent bridge imported-source syncs for the same vault/config through index refresh while keying on bridge artifact-discovery inputs such as `agents.defaults.workspace`.
- Preserve existing vault safety boundaries for symlink/path-alias and directory collision cases.

Related to #91154.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Local memory-wiki bridge imports no longer run duplicate imported-source sync/import passes for concurrent callers of the same vault/config, the shared sync covers downstream index refresh under gateway load, the coalescing key separates callers with different default workspaces, and imported source page writes retry transient fs-safe `path-mismatch` without weakening existing symlink/path safety checks.

**Environment tested:** Linux worktree at `/media/vdb/migrated-from-root-media-vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-91154`, Node 22.22.0, pnpm 11.2.2. Verification used local filesystem-backed memory-wiki vault/workspace fixtures only; no external channel credentials or live Feishu/Slack/IRC transport were used.

**Command run after the patch:**

```text
- extensions/memory-wiki/src/source-page-shared.ts

- extensions/memory-wiki/src/bridge.ts

- extensions/memory-wiki/src/source-sync.ts
- extensions/memory-wiki/src/source-sync.test.ts
```

```text
oxfmt --check: All matched files use the correct format.
```

**Observed result after fix:** Concurrent imported-source sync callers shared a single in-flight import pass (`concurrentImportedSourceListings: 1`) and still returned the imported bridge page. A settled follow-up sync performed a fresh listing and skipped the unchanged page. The production-path proof also replaced the imported page with a vault symlink and confirmed the sync was refused while the symlink target remained unchanged. The gateway-load proof exercised the production gateway registration path with 60 concurrent `wiki.palace`, `wiki.importInsights`, and `wiki.status` calls against 13 local bridge artifacts; the shared imported-source sync produced exactly one bridge import listing, one ingest log, and one compile log while all 60 gateway responses succeeded and `wiki.status` observed 13 bridge source pages. The focused regression tests cover the transient `path-mismatch` retry path, confirm the imported page body plus sync state are written after retry, verify a recovered bridge page write is counted as an import (`importedCount: 1`, `skippedCount: 0`), confirm concurrent bridge imported-source sync plus index refresh is coalesced for the same vault, and confirm concurrent bridge syncs stay separate when `agents.defaults.workspace` differs.

**What was not tested:** This does not claim a full #91154 product-policy resolution. It does not test live Feishu/Slack/IRC or any other non-local channel transport, RSS import spirals, broader batching/backpressure policy beyond same-vault gateway coalescing, partial-failure product behavior, or maintainer-only load environments. Full-repo `tsc --noEmit --pretty false` was attempted earlier but the local process was terminated by the environment after heap/OOM pressure, so the passing checks here are the focused Vitest, targeted oxlint, targeted oxfmt check, and local production-path proofs above.

target.title: fix #63343: Browser bridge tab not found race
target.body:
Fixes #63343.

## Summary

- Preserve the `openTab("about:blank")` result in `ensureTabAvailable()` so a newly created target remains selectable while CDP `/json/list` is still catching up.
- For local-managed profiles, wait through the existing tab discovery window when the `wsUrl` filter would otherwise remove every selectable tab.
- Add regression coverage for the discarded-opened-target race, delayed `webSocketDebuggerUrl` discovery, and the bounded unfiltered fallback.

## Root Cause

- **Fix classification:** Root-cause browser selection bug fix for the shared `ensureTabAvailable()` state resolver.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High. The change is limited to one source file and one focused regression test file, and it is backed by local production-path proof plus the two ClawSweeper-requested browser test commands.
- **Root cause:** The source of the crash-loop is that `ensureTabAvailable()` opened a valid `about:blank` target when the first list was empty, then discarded that returned target and immediately rebuilt candidates only from the next `/json/list` snapshot. For local-managed profiles, that second snapshot was also filtered by `wsUrl`, so a target that existed but had not yet appeared in `/json/list` or had not yet received `webSocketDebuggerUrl` was treated as no tab at all.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The invariant that was broken is shared tab selection state: after `ensureTabAvailable()` creates or sees a selectable target, the resolver must not lose that target solely because CDP target metadata is lagging. Preserving the opened snapshot, polling for delayed `wsUrl`, and only then falling back to the unfiltered selectable snapshot fixes the source selection invariant rather than masking individual browser actions downstream.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** This removes a false `BrowserTabNotFoundError` path that can stop browser tools during cold start or after a transient Playwright/CDP hiccup even though Chrome has a usable tab target.

## What changed

- `extensions/browser/src/browser/server-context.selection.ts` now keeps the opened tab snapshot and merges it into the next selection snapshot when CDP has not listed it yet.
- The local-managed `wsUrl` candidate filter now performs bounded polling using the existing browser tab discovery constants.
- If polling expires and selectable tabs still exist without `wsUrl`, selection falls back to those unfiltered tabs so downstream paths can continue with the known target id.
- `extensions/browser/src/browser/server-context.tab-selection-state.test.ts` adds focused regressions for the three timing states reported in the issue.

## What did NOT change

- **What did NOT change:** Out of scope: no browser route API, config, schema, protocol, wire format, defaults, navigation policy, or public command behavior changed. `focusTab()` and `closeTab()` still resolve against the current tab list, remote Playwright profiles and Chrome MCP existing-session profiles keep their prior behavior, and browser-internal target filtering remains owned by `listTabs()` / `isSelectableCdpBrowserTarget()`.

## Architecture / source-of-truth check

- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The canonical source-of-truth for default browser target selection is `createProfileSelectionOps()` in `server-context.selection.ts`, so the fix stays in that resolver instead of adding catch/fallback behavior to individual browser actions. Public contract scanning found no related open PR for #63343, and this patch does not introduce schema, config, migration, or protocol changes.
- **Patch quality notes:** The patch changes one production selection invariant and one regression test file. The `as unknown as Response` values are test-only fetch fixtures matching the existing harness style in this file; they do not add production type escapes. The public-contract and merge-risk signals are explained here and below because the touched selection path affects browser availability/session state, but no public contract surface is expanded.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `ensureTabAvailable()` no longer reports `BrowserTabNotFoundError` when CDP target discovery or `webSocketDebuggerUrl` discovery lags a valid local-managed browser tab.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux local OpenClaw worktree at `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-63343`, Node with repository dependencies installed from the frozen lockfile.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node --import tsx /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-63343-evidence/local-browser-selection-proof.mjs`
- **Evidence after fix:**

```json
{
  "issue": 63343,
  "proof": "production createProfileSelectionOps local behavior",
  "scenarios": [
    {
      "name": "openTab_return_preserved_when_json_list_lags",
      "selectedTargetId": "OPENED_FROM_JSON_NEW",
      "selectedHasWsUrl": true,
      "listCount": 2,
      "openCount": 1
    },
    {
      "name": "wsUrl_discovery_poll_keeps_local_candidate",
      "selectedTargetId": "WS_DISCOVERED_AFTER_POLL",
      "selectedHasWsUrl": true,
      "listCount": 3
    },
    {
      "name": "unfiltered_fallback_after_wsUrl_poll_exhaustion",
      "selectedTargetId": "UNFILTERED_AFTER_POLL_EXHAUSTED",
      "selectedHasWsUrl": false,
      "listCount": 22,
      "elapsedMs": 2009
    }
  ],
  "result": "passed"
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** All three local selection race scenarios selected a concrete target id and updated `lastTargetId`; none produced `BrowserTabNotFoundError`.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a live macOS Chrome session. The reported failure is in the shared TypeScript selection path, and the local proof above calls the production `createProfileSelectionOps()` code directly for the relevant CDP timing states.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `extensions/browser/src/browser/server-context.tab-selection-state.test.ts` locks the local-managed race behavior; `extensions/browser/src/browser/server-context.remote-profile-tab-ops.playwright.test.ts` was rerun to verify remote Playwright tab selection remains unchanged.
- **Scenario locked in:** The regression tests cover cold start where `/json/list` remains empty after `openTab()`, delayed `webSocketDebuggerUrl` population where the third list call supplies `wsUrl`, and a persistent `wsUrl` lag where the bounded polling window expires and the unfiltered selectable target is returned.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The tests exercise `ensureTabAvailable()` through the existing browser route harness and the production selection resolver, so they fail on the original discarded-target/filtering race without adding a broader browser integration harness or changing unrelated tab operations.

## Verification

- `node --import tsx /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-63343-evidence/local-browser-selection-proof.mjs` — passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/browser/src/browser/server-context.tab-selection-state.test.ts` — 17 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/browser/src/browser/server-context.remote-profile-tab-ops.playwright.test.ts` — 11 tests passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/browser/src/browser/server-context.selection.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/server-context.tab-selection-state.test.ts` — passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/browser/src/browser/server-context.selection.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/server-context.tab-selection-state.test.ts` — passed.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: availability` and `merge-risk: session-state` are the relevant categories because this code chooses the browser target used by subsequent tool actions and updates `lastTargetId`.
- **Risk explanation:** The changed path only runs inside `ensureTabAvailable()`. It preserves a target that was just opened by the same resolver, waits for CDP to fill in `wsUrl` only when the local-managed filter would otherwise leave zero candidates, and falls back only to selectable tabs already returned by `listTabs()`.
- **Why acceptable:** The patch narrows the failure mode from false `tab not found` to selecting an already-created or already-listed selectable tab. It does not alter route inputs, config defaults, target creation, target filtering, or remote-driver behavior, and the regression tests plus local production-path proof cover the availability/session-state edge cases introduced by the change.

target.title: refactor: add session accessor seam with gateway consumer
target.body:
## Summary

Adds the storage-neutral session accessor seam and routes the first gateway session-entry production reads through it.

## Why

This intentionally supersedes #88840 and #89120 as a single review unit. Reviewed in isolation, #88840 introduces the accessor before any production caller uses it, so the repository dead-code gate correctly reports `src/config/sessions/session-accessor.ts` as unused. Bundling the first non-SDK gateway consumer keeps the branch-by-abstraction shape while making the new seam production-reachable without adding temporary dead-code allowlist debt.

## Changes

- Add session accessor contract
- Add file-backed accessor implementation
- Route gateway entry reads
- Preserve legacy lookup behavior
- Update focused seam tests

## Verification

- `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs`
- `pnpm deadcode:dependencies`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts src/config/sessions/store-read.test.ts src/config/sessions/store-writer.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-history-http.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-resolve.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/config/sessions/combined-store-gateway.ts src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts src/config/sessions/session-accessor.ts src/config/sessions/store.runtime.ts src/config/sessions/store.ts src/config/sessions/transcript-append.ts src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts src/gateway/session-utils.ts src/gateway/sessions-resolve.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-resolve.ts src/plugin-sdk/session-store-runtime.ts`
- `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --prompt ...`

Known unrelated current-main drift: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/pdf-tool.model-config.test.ts` fails because the unchanged PDF model-config test still expects `openai/gpt-5.4-mini` while current media defaults resolve `openai/gpt-5.5`.

Refs #88838. Supersedes #88840 and #89120.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Gateway session entry reads, session resolution, entry creation, and the routed transcript-append path must behave identically through the new storage-neutral session accessor seam introduced by this PR (seam + first production gateway consumer).
- Real environment tested: Maintainer's live macOS gateway (launchd service `ai.openclaw.gateway`, port 18789) built from this branch in `~/Projects/clawdbot`, running against the real `~/.openclaw` state directory: 14 existing sessions, live Telegram and Discord channels, 45 active cron jobs. Branch was merged into current `main` locally before building.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm build`; `launchctl bootstrap gui/$UID ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ai.openclaw.gateway.plist`; `node dist/index.js gateway status`; `node dist/index.js sessions`; `node dist/index.js agent --agent main -m "Live seam proof probe for PR 90463. Reply with exactly one word: pong" --json`; direct inspection of `~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json` and the newly created transcript JSONL.
- Evidence after fix: Live terminal output and runtime logs. Gateway log shows `http server listening (11 plugins…; 3.5s)` then `gateway ready`. Seam-routed listing printed `Sessions listed: 14`, matching the 14 entries in the on-disk store snapshot taken before the restart. Live turn output excerpt:

  ```
  "status": "ok",
  "payloads": [ { "text": "pong", "mediaUrl": null } ],
  "sessionId": "538d6092-6323-4fcc-a7d0-d4f92a8b9bf1",
  "sessionFile": ".../agents/main/sessions/538d6092-6323-4fcc-a7d0-d4f92a8b9bf1.jsonl"
  ```

  The new transcript contains a valid `session` header with the matching id plus exactly the user and assistant records carrying the probe text and the `pong` reply. The `agent:main:main` entry was created with matching `sessionId`/`sessionFile` and fresh `updatedAt`. All 14 pre-existing store keys remain present afterward with no unattributable field changes.
- Observed result after fix: Seam-routed gateway reads and writes produce the same observable artifacts and behavior as the previous direct store access — same store file, same entry shapes, same transcript format. One ERROR-level runtime log line occurred during the soak (a transient Telegram `Bad Gateway` 502; ingress self-recovered; unrelated to this change).
- What was not tested: Channel-inbound end-to-end delivery (message in via Telegram → reply out) beyond ingress startup; concurrent CLI-plus-gateway writer contention; Windows.

target.title: fix(exec): preserve approved gateway output
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserves multiline output for approved async gateway exec follow-ups instead of tailing it to the compact background-notification limit.
- Keeps ordinary background `notifyOnExit` summaries on the existing compact formatter, so this does not expand normal background channel notifications.
- Carries the exec capture `truncated` state into approval follow-up formatting and adds an explicit truncation notice only when the capture cap was actually hit.
- Adds regression coverage for a Discord-routed async approval follow-up with more than 400 characters of multiline output.

## Linked context

Closes #41152

Related #41170

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request. This follows the source-level diagnosis and patch boundary described in #41152.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: async approved gateway exec follow-ups were using the compact background notification formatter, which tailed output to the last 400 characters and normalized whitespace before resuming the agent.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Windows, running the repository's exec approval flow tests against the TypeScript source.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec.approval-id.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts
```

- Evidence after fix: the new regression test `preserves multiline approved gateway exec output in async follow-ups` runs an approved gateway exec command that emits 60 newline-separated lines, then verifies the follow-up message sent to the resumed agent contains the first line, the last line, and the original newline boundary between the first two lines.
- Observed result after fix: the approved gateway exec follow-up preserves multiline output instead of flattening it or dropping the front of the output at the 400-character tail boundary.
- What was not tested: a live Discord approval round trip with a real Discord account.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this is local source-level proof through the existing exec approval follow-up path; I did not have a live Discord approval environment available here.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): current `main` formats non-diagnostics approval completions with `normalizeNotifyOutput(tail(..., DEFAULT_NOTIFY_TAIL_CHARS))`, matching the reported 400-character flattened tail behavior.

## Tests and validation

target.title: feat(feishu): extract option, options, form_value from card action callbacks
target.body:
## Problem

When users interact with Feishu interactive card components like `select_static` (dropdowns), `checkbox` (multi-select), or `form` (form submissions), the selected values are lost because `handleFeishuCardAction` only extracts data from `action.value`.

Feishu sends selection data in separate fields:
- `action.option` — for `select_static` single selection
- `action.options` — for `checkbox` / `multi_select` multiple selections  
- `action.form_value` — for `form` submission data

These fields are completely ignored by the current implementation, making it impossible for AI Agents to know what the user actually selected.

Related issue: #42754

## Solution

1. **Extended `FeishuCardActionEvent` type** to include `option`, `options`, and `form_value` fields
2. **Updated content extraction logic** to merge these fields with `action.value` when present, using priority: `form_value` > `options` > `option` > existing behavior
3. **Maintained full backward compatibility** — existing button interactions (`value.text`, `value.command`) work exactly as before

## Changes

- `extensions/feishu/src/card-action.ts` — Type definition + extraction logic (14 lines changed)
- `extensions/feishu/src/bot.card-action.test.ts` — 4 new test cases (90 lines added)

## Test Plan

- [x] `select_static` with `option` field — value merged into JSON output
- [x] `checkbox` with `options` array — values merged into JSON output  
- [x] `form` with `form_value` object — values merged into JSON output
- [x] Existing button with `value.command` — backward compatible, unchanged behavior
- [x] All 6 tests pass (`vitest`)
- [x] TypeScript compilation passes

## AI Disclosure

This PR was authored with assistance from an AI coding assistant (Claude/Cursor). All code changes have been reviewed and tested.

target.title: Node run/install: add --header for WebSocket upgrade (Cloudflare Zero Trust)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `openclaw node run` and `node install` had no way to pass custom HTTP headers on the WebSocket upgrade request, causing silent 302 redirects and connection failures when the node gateway sits behind Cloudflare Zero Trust or similar auth proxies
- **Why it matters:** Teams deploying OpenClaw in Zero Trust network environments (common in enterprise) were completely blocked — the WS handshake would redirect instead of upgrading, with no workaround
- **What changed:** Added a repeatable `--header "Name: Value"` CLI flag to both `node run` and `node install`; headers are persisted to config and forwarded to the gateway WebSocket client; also supports `OPENCLAW_NODE_HEADERS`, `CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_ID`, and `CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_SECRET` env vars for the installed daemon service
- **What did NOT change:** No changes to gateway routing logic, auth token handling, skill execution, or any behavior for users not passing `--header`

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #42792
- Related: none

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `openclaw node run` now accepts repeatable `--header "Name: Value"` flags
- `openclaw node install` now accepts repeatable `--header "Name: Value"` flags; headers are written to config and used by the installed daemon on startup
- New env vars supported: `OPENCLAW_NODE_HEADERS` (JSON), `CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_ID`, `CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_SECRET`
- No behavior change for users who do not pass `--header`

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `Yes` — headers may contain auth tokens; persisted to existing node config file (same location/permissions as all other config)
- New/changed network calls? `Yes` — user-supplied headers forwarded on outbound WebSocket upgrade request
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `No`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- **Risk + mitigation:** Headers containing secrets are written to the existing node config file (not a new location). No headers are logged or surfaced in output. The `CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_SECRET` env var name makes sensitivity clear to users.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 15.x (Apple Silicon)
- Runtime/container: Node.js v22, pnpm monorepo
- Model/provider: N/A (gateway connectivity issue, not model-specific)
- Integration/channel: Cloudflare Zero Trust (simulated via mock HTTP proxy)
- Relevant config: none required

### Steps

**1. Start a mock proxy that returns 302 if the CF header is absent:**
```js
const http = require('http');
const server = http.createServer((req, res) => {
  if (!req.headers['cf-access-client-id']) {
    res.writeHead(302, { Location: 'https://example.cloudflareaccess.com' });
    res.end();
    return;
  }
  res.writeHead(200);
  res.end();
});
server.listen(9999, () => console.log('Mock proxy listening on 9999'));
```

**2. Without fix — run in second terminal:**
```bash
pnpm openclaw node run --host localhost --port 9999
```

**3. With fix — run with headers:**
```bash
pnpm openclaw node run --host localhost --port 9999 \
  --header "CF-Access-Client-Id: test123" \
  --header "CF-Access-Client-Secret: secret456"
```

### Expected

- WS upgrade request includes supplied headers
- No 302 redirect when correct Zero Trust headers are present

### Actual (before fix)

- WS upgrade sent with no custom headers → Cloudflare returns 302 → `Unexpected server response: 302`

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets

**Before fix:**
```
Unexpected server response: 302
```

**After fix:**
```
Unexpected server response: 200
```
_(200 is expected — mock is not a real WS server; the point is the 302 redirect is gone)_

New unit tests added:
- `src/gateway/client.test.ts` — headers passed to WS constructor
- `src/daemon/program-args.test.ts` — `--header` args emitted correctly
- `src/cli/node-cli/header-args.test.ts` — parse logic including edge cases

All 895 test files, 7301 tests passing. `pnpm check` clean.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios:**
  - `node run` with single `--header` → header present in WS upgrade
  - `node run` with multiple `--header` flags → all headers forwarded
  - `node run` with no `--header` → behavior identical to pre-fix
  - Mock proxy 302→200 transition confirmed end-to-end
  - `pnpm check` and `pnpm test` both clean on branch
- **Edge cases checked:**
  - Malformed header string (missing `:`) → `parseHeaderArgs` throws with clear error
  - Header value containing `:` (e.g. URLs) → splits on first colon only, value preserved
  - Empty header value → accepted (valid HTTP)
- **What I did not verify:**
  - Live Cloudflare Zero Trust environment (tested via mock proxy only)
  - `node install` daemon persistence across system restarts
  - Windows path behavior for config persistence

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — `--header` is purely additive; existing behavior unchanged
- Config/env changes? `Yes` — new optional `headers` field in node config; old configs without it continue to work
- Migration needed? `No`

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert: remove `--header` flags from invocation, or delete `headers` key from node config file
- Files/config to restore: `~/.openclaw/node-config.json` — remove the `headers` field
- Known bad symptoms: WS connections failing after upgrade if headers are malformed; `parseHeaderArgs` throws at startup with a descriptive error

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** User accidentally persists a sensitive token to disk via `node install --header`
  - **Mitigation:** Consistent with how other sensitive config (API keys) is already handled in node config. No new attack surface; file permissions unchanged.
- **Risk:** Header injection via malformed `--header` value
  - **Mitigation:** `parseHeaderArgs` validates format; `ws` library handles header serialization — no raw string concatenation into HTTP stream.

target.title: fix(logging): prune non-idle stale diagnostic session states (fixes #91697)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: `pruneDiagnosticSessionStates()` only cleans up diagnostic entries where `state === "idle"` AND `queueDepth <= 0`. Entries that never transition back to "idle" — for example after a failed stuck-session recovery — accumulate in the process-local Map indefinitely, holding activity snapshots, tool call history, and queue metadata in V8 heap until the 2000-entry FIFO cap is reached.
- **Root Cause**: When `requestStuckSessionRecovery()` fails (e.g. stale generation check, already-closed session), `recoveryOutcomeMutatesSessionState()` returns `false` for `status: "failed"` outcomes, so `applyRecoveryOutcomeToDiagnosticState()` never sets the diagnostic state back to "idle". The prune guard `isIdle && queueDepth <= 0 && ageMs > TTL` then skips these orphaned non-idle entries forever.
- **Fix**: Add a fallback deletion branch in `pruneDiagnosticSessionStates()` that removes any non-idle entry older than `SESSION_STATE_TTL_MS` (30 min), regardless of state. The existing idle+empty-queue path is unchanged; the new `else if (!isIdle)` branch catches ghost entries that survived failed recovery.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/logging/diagnostic-session-state.ts` — added early `continue` for entries within TTL, then `else if (!isIdle)` fallback delete in the prune loop
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - `recoveryOutcomeMutatesSessionState()` — not changed; recovery coordinator behavior is left alone to avoid side effects
  - The `SESSION_STATE_MAX_ENTRIES` FIFO cap — remains as a hard upper bound (2000 entries)

### Reproduction

1. Gateway with `diagnostics.stuckSessionAbortMs` configured (e.g. 300000)
2. A session stalls → `stuckSessionAbortMs` fires → recovery attempts
3. Recovery fails (e.g. stale generation check `isDiagnosticSessionStateCurrent` returns false)
4. Outcome `{ status: "failed", action: "none" }` skips the idle transition
5. **Before this PR**: entry stays in "processing" state in the Map forever (up to the 2000-entry cap), never pruned by the 30-min TTL sweep
6. **After this PR**: entry is removed once its `lastActivity` age exceeds `SESSION_STATE_TTL_MS` (30 min)

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed (91697)**: The diagnostic session state Map accumulates non-idle ghost entries indefinitely after failed stuck-session recoveries. After the fix, any non-idle entry older than the 30-minute TTL is cleaned up on the next prune sweep, preventing unbounded heap growth from orphaned diagnostic state.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22 — Vitest with fake timers against `pruneDiagnosticSessionStates` and `getDiagnosticSessionState`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668001395/openclawProject1

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  69 passed (69)
   Start at  22:18:16
   Duration  2.40s (transform 982ms, setup 243ms, import 1.77s, tests 209ms, environment 0ms)
```

**Observed result after fix**: All 69 diagnostic tests pass, including the new "evicts stale non-idle session states (ghost entries after failed recovery)" test. The test creates a non-idle ("processing") entry with a 31-minute-old `lastActivity` timestamp and a non-zero `queueDepth`, then triggers a prune via `getDiagnosticSessionState`. After the prune sweep, the ghost entry is deleted and only the fresh entry remains. The existing "evicts stale idle session states" test continues to pass unchanged, confirming the idle-path behavior is preserved.

**What was not tested**: Live Gateway integration was not driven — the test uses Vitest fake timers to simulate the TTL window and does not exercise the full stuck-session recovery→failed→prune pipeline end-to-end with a real Gateway process.

**Repro confirmation**: The new test case fails on origin/main before the patch — non-idle stale entries survive the prune sweep — and passes after the fix, confirming the ghost entry is cleaned up once it exceeds the TTL regardless of state.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: A genuinely long-running session that stays "processing" for >30 minutes could be incorrectly pruned.
  **Mitigation**: The `lastActivity` field is updated on every diagnostic heartbeat (not wall-clock time since state transition). A genuinely active processing session refreshes `lastActivity` continually, so `ageMs` stays well under 30 minutes. Only entries with no activity for >30 minutes are evicted.
- **Risk**: The fallback branch could mask a recovery coordinator bug by silently cleaning up entries that recovery should have handled.
  **Mitigation**: The prune sweep is a garbage-collection concern, not an error-handling concern. Recovery coordinator bugs should be fixed separately; this change only ensures memory doesn't grow unbounded when they occur.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] agents (diagnostic session state tracking)
- [x] logging

### Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts` — covers prune behavior for idle and non-idle stale entries
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic-stability-bundle.test.ts` — covers downstream diagnostic stability snapshot consumers

### Review Findings Addressed

N/A (initial submission)

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #91697

target.title: fix(gateway): hide stream-error sentinel from chat history
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Chat history could display the canonical stream-error sentinel `[assistant turn failed before producing content]` as a visible assistant row before the real assistant response.
- **Solution:** Treat a pure sentinel assistant message as display-only synthetic content only when the row carries stream-error provenance.
- **What changed:** `src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts` now gates sentinel suppression on error provenance (`stopReason: "error"` or `internalStreamError`) plus pure text-only content, and `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts` now locks both the drop case and the ordinary exact-text preservation case.
- **What did NOT change:** This PR does not change provider replay, session repair, the canonical sentinel constant, persisted stream-error assistant message construction, message delivery, persistence format, schema, migration, or default persisted content behavior.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Users see the actual assistant response without a synthetic streaming-layer sentinel in chat history, while a legitimate assistant reply that exactly matches the sentinel text remains visible.
- **Fixes #91503:** This PR fixes the reported chat-history display artifact.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Chat history display hides a pure stream-error sentinel assistant row only when it has stream-error provenance, while preserving an ordinary assistant message whose text exactly equals `[assistant turn failed before producing content]`.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Without the provenance guard, a real assistant response that happens to match the sentinel text could disappear from displayed chat history; with the guard, synthetic stream-error rows are hidden and ordinary model text remains visible.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux checkout of this PR on Node.js v22.22.0 using OpenClaw production TypeScript modules via `node --import tsx`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```text
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91573
node --import tsx /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91573-evidence/stream-error-sentinel-provenance-proof.mjs
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/agent-prompt.test.ts src/agents/stream-message-shared.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --deny-warnings src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts
git diff --check
```
- **Evidence after fix:**
```text
Behavior addressed: chat history display hides only stream-error fallback sentinel rows with error provenance.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw TypeScript production modules under node --import tsx on Linux.
Exact path exercised: sanitizeChatHistoryMessages from src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts.
sentinel_text=[assistant turn failed before producing content]
stream_error_input=assistant row with stopReason=error and errorMessage
ordinary_input=assistant row with exact sentinel text and no stream-error provenance
projected_messages=[{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"hello"}],"timestamp":1},{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"[assistant turn failed before producing content]"}],"timestamp":3},{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"actual fallback response"}],"timestamp":4}]
ordinary_only_messages=[{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"[assistant turn failed before producing content]"}],"timestamp":3}]
ASSERT_PASS: provenanced stream-error sentinel is hidden while ordinary exact sentinel assistant text remains visible
```
- **Observed result after fix:** The production display projection removed the assistant row with `stopReason="error"` and `errorMessage`, preserved the ordinary exact sentinel assistant row without stream-error provenance, and kept the later real assistant response visible.
- **What was not tested:** Live external provider streaming failure was not forced; the changed contract is the local shared chat-history display projection only, not provider transport, provider replay, message delivery, or session repair.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts`, `src/gateway/agent-prompt.test.ts`, and `src/agents/stream-message-shared.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** A persisted history containing a user message, a stream-error assistant sentinel row, and a later real assistant response projects to the user message plus the real response; a separate ordinary assistant row whose text exactly equals the sentinel stays visible.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The server-methods coverage exercises the shared chat-history sanitization owner directly, while the prompt and stream-message shards guard adjacent sentinel provenance behavior that ClawSweeper requested.

```text
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 28.53s
src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts: 1 file passed, 127 tests passed

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 33.41s
src/gateway/agent-prompt.test.ts and src/agents/stream-message-shared.test.ts: 5 files passed, 51 tests passed

Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.
```

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** Fixed in the PR branch by addressing the author-side ClawSweeper findings; the `status: ⏳ waiting on author` label should be recomputed after the updated branch is reviewed.
- **RF-002:** Fixed by adding `preserves ordinary assistant messages matching the stream-error sentinel text` in `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts`.
- **RF-003:** Fixed by requiring stream-error provenance before dropping the sentinel, so legitimate exact sentinel assistant text without provenance remains visible.
- **RF-004:** Fixed with a narrow mechanical repair in the changed projection predicate and adjacent test only.
- **RF-005:** Fixed in `src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts` by gating sentinel suppression on `stopReason === "error"` or `internalStreamError === true`.
- **RF-006:** Verified with `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts`, which passed 127 tests.
- **RF-007:** Verified with `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/agent-prompt.test.ts src/agents/stream-message-shared.test.ts`, which passed both requested shards.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** message-delivery and availability were reviewed because the files live under gateway display projection and assistant message history handling.
- **Risk explanation:** The merge risk is bounded to display projection: the patch drops only an assistant message whose trimmed text exactly equals the canonical stream-error sentinel, whose content has no non-text payload, and whose record carries stream-error provenance. It does not route, deliver, retry, or persist messages differently.
- **Why acceptable:** The diff is XS, source/test paired, and verified with a local production-path proof plus the two ClawSweeper-requested Vitest shards. Compatibility/default behavior is preserved for replay/session repair because persisted sentinel content remains unchanged and only transformed display output is filtered.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The root source of the bug is a contract split: `STREAM_ERROR_FALLBACK_TEXT` is canonical replay-safety state stored in assistant `content` when streaming fails before model content exists, but the shared chat-history display projection originally did not hide that synthetic row, and the first PR version hid by text equality without checking provenance.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The source-of-truth split is raw persisted stream-error content versus transformed display projection. This patch fixes the projection owner that prepares user-visible history and preserves the persisted sentinel path before provider replay or session repair consumes it.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause display projection fix.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High: the repair is limited to the predicate ClawSweeper flagged, includes the requested negative test, and is backed by local production-module proof plus the requested test shards.
- **Patch quality notes:** No unrelated refactor, dependency change, lockfile change, public API change, schema change, config change, protocol change, or channel/provider behavior change.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The canonical source-of-truth sentinel remains `STREAM_ERROR_FALLBACK_TEXT` in `src/agents/stream-message-shared.ts`; `src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts` remains the transformed display projection owner. Out of scope / not changed: provider replay, session repair, message delivery, persistence format, schema, migration, and default persisted content behavior.

target.title: [codex] fix gateway hot-mode restart reloads
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a stale runtime-config hole in gateway config reloads: when `gateway.reload.mode` is `hot` and a config file change requires a gateway restart, the reloader now schedules the existing restart path instead of warning and returning.

This prevents doctor/configure or external config writes from leaving the live gateway on stale model/provider/auth runtime state after disk config has been repaired.

## Details

- In hot mode, restart-required reload plans now log `hot mode scheduling restart` and call the existing `onRestart` path.
- Existing idle-aware restart machinery remains responsible for deferring or forcing the process restart.
- Added a regression test for `gateway.auth.token` changes in hot mode so restart-required auth changes cannot silently stay stale.
- Added a regression test for doctor-style OpenAI Codex model route repairs so model route changes still hot-reload when no restart-only path is present.
- Updated gateway reload docs so `hot` mode no longer promises restart-required changes will be ignored.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: in hot reload mode, when a config write touches any restart-required key (e.g. `auth.profiles.*`, `models.pricing`, `gateway.*`, etc.), the gateway's reload runtime dropped the entire plan with a single `log.warn` and returned. The result was the gateway kept running with the previous snapshot in memory while the on-disk config had already been repaired. Observed in production on the deployment fork as a 4-hour silent outage where the in-process agent kept resolving the legacy `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` model id after `openclaw doctor --fix` had migrated the on-disk config to `openai/gpt-5.5`. Group Telegram messages silently dropped while admin DMs returned a canned `[agent failed]` reply that masked the failure.

- **Real environment tested**: deployed `openclaw` gateway on `gateway-host` (macOS, launchd-supervised, KeepAlive=dict { Crashed=true, SuccessfulExit=false }) running `upgrade-v2026.6.2` deployment fork with this PR's commit cherry-picked, build commit `421a97cf2d`. Reload-event capture below via `ssh user@gateway-host` against `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log` and `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log`.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  ssh user@gateway-host 'cp -p ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json.bak'
  ssh user@gateway-host 'jq ".gateway.reload.mode = \"hot\"" ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json > /tmp/c.json && mv /tmp/c.json ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json'
  ssh user@gateway-host 'jq ".models.pricing = {enabled: true}" ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json > /tmp/c.json && mv /tmp/c.json ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json'
  ssh user@gateway-host 'grep -E "hot mode scheduling restart|hot mode ignoring" ~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log | tail -5'
  ssh user@gateway-host 'cp ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json.bak ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json'
  ssh user@gateway-host 'launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/ai.openclaw.gateway'
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**: Captured live from `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log` and `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log` on `gateway-host` via `ssh user@gateway-host` against the deployed `openclaw` gateway on commit `421a97cf2d` (PID <gateway-pid> at capture time, restarted to 93522 after the proof).

  ```
  $ grep -E "hot mode (ignoring|scheduling restart)" ~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log | tail -5
  2026-04-22T14:18:06.576-04:00 [reload] config reload requires gateway restart; hot mode ignoring (plugins.entries.whatsapp.enabled)
  2026-04-24T08:34:56.747-04:00 [reload] config reload requires gateway restart; hot mode ignoring (plugins.entries.openai.config, plugins.entries.acpx.config.timeoutSeconds)
  2026-06-02T10:10:59.246-04:00 [reload] config reload requires gateway restart; hot mode ignoring (auth.profiles.openai-codex:user@example.com, auth.profiles.openai:user@example.com)
  2026-06-02T18:11:56.785-04:00 [reload] config reload requires gateway restart; hot mode scheduling restart (models.pricing)
  ```

  The first three lines are historical pre-fix instances of the silent-staleness mode this PR addresses, including the 2026-06-02T10:10:59Z production incident that motivated the patch (`auth.profiles.openai-codex:...` rename triggered `hot mode ignoring`; the runtime kept the stale `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` model id for 4 hours until `launchctl kickstart -k` was run by hand). The fourth line is the after-fix capture from a controlled hot-mode + restart-required write to `models.pricing.enabled` — the new `hot mode scheduling restart` text appears and the restart path is actually called:

  ```
  $ awk '/2026-06-02T18:11:5[6-7]/' ~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log
  2026-06-02T18:11:56.784-04:00 [reload] config change detected; evaluating reload (models.pricing)
  2026-06-02T18:11:56.943-04:00 [gateway] signal SIGUSR1 received
  2026-06-02T18:11:56.943-04:00 [gateway] received SIGUSR1; restarting
  2026-06-02T18:11:56.955-04:00 [shutdown] started: gateway restarting
  2026-06-02T18:11:56.955-04:00 [plugins] memory-lancedb: stopped
  2026-06-02T18:11:57.097-04:00 [gmail-watcher] gmail watcher stopped
  2026-06-02T18:11:57.098-04:00 [shutdown] completed cleanly in 144ms
  2026-06-02T18:11:57.104-04:00 [gateway] restart mode: full process restart (supervisor restart)
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: The patched `hot` branch fires correctly. `[reload]` detects the change, emits the new `hot mode scheduling restart (models.pricing)` warn line, the existing idle-aware restart machinery sends SIGUSR1, the gateway runs a clean shutdown, and `[gateway] restart mode: full process restart (supervisor restart)` records the handoff to the supervisor for respawn. The 4-hour silent staleness failure mode (`hot mode ignoring`) is replaced with a single restart cycle.

  **Honest follow-up finding from this capture**: on `gateway-host` the launchd plist uses `KeepAlive: { Crashed=true, SuccessfulExit=false }` to short-circuit the historical zombie-on-port cascade from the isolated-session SIGKILL path. The patched supervisor-restart hand-off exits with code 0 ("completed cleanly"), and launchd's `SuccessfulExit=false` then refuses to respawn — the gateway stays down until `launchctl kickstart -k` runs externally. This is not a regression introduced by this PR (the old `hot mode ignoring` path also left the gateway stale), but maintainers should know: deployment hosts that use `KeepAlive: { SuccessfulExit: false }` to dodge a different failure cascade need a separate kickstart-on-clean-exit watcher to make this PR's `queueRestart` self-heal end-to-end. The deployment fork ships such a watcher as a follow-up; the patch itself remains correct.

- **What was not tested**: the auto-respawn end-to-end behavior on `KeepAlive=true` hosts (or hosts that wrap the gateway in a non-launchd supervisor) was not driven from this PR; the change was verified by triggering the SIGUSR1 + clean-shutdown sequence and observing the patched warn line and the existing restart-path call. A live Telegram round-trip immediately following the restart was not driven to keep the disruption window short (config was reverted in under 90s; gateway was kickstarted back to PID <gateway-pid-after-restart> with `agent model: openai/gpt-5.5` and `/health` returning 200 as before).



## Operator-side answer to the launchd-respawn gap

The "honest follow-up" section above flagged that hosts using `KeepAlive: { SuccessfulExit: false }` to dodge a separate failure cascade do not respawn the gateway after this PR's clean-exit restart hand-off. To keep the patched gateway self-healing on those hosts, the deployment fork ships a separate LaunchAgent watchdog (`com.openclaw.gateway-respawn-watchdog`) that runs on a 30-second interval, checks both `launchctl print` state AND port-18789 binding, and calls `launchctl kickstart -k` only when both indicate the gateway is fully down. The double-condition gate prevents collisions with the isolated-session SIGKILL respawn cascade that motivated the original `KeepAlive=dict` policy.

Watchdog is independent of this PR's diff (it lives in the deploy-fork host config, not in the openclaw runtime), but documenting it here so the launchd-respawn gap clawsweeper flagged is paired with the production-deploy fix that closes it end-to-end:

```
$ ssh user@gateway-host '/bin/launchctl print "gui/$(id -u)/com.openclaw.gateway-respawn-watchdog" | grep -E "state|run interval"'
        state = not running
        run interval = 30 seconds
                state = active
                state = active

$ ssh user@gateway-host 'cat ~/bin/openclaw-gateway-respawn-watchdog.sh | head -30'
#!/bin/bash
# Respawn watchdog for the openclaw gateway. Runs every 30s via launchd
# StartInterval. If the gateway is "not running" AND nothing is bound to
# port 18789, kickstart it. Both conditions must hold to avoid colliding
# with the isolated-session SIGKILL respawn cascade or with operator-driven
# transient stops.
```

Verified end to end: a controlled hot-mode + restart-required write triggered the new `hot mode scheduling restart (models.pricing)` log line, the gateway's existing supervised-restart machinery logged `restart mode: full process restart (supervisor restart)` and clean-exited, and the watchdog noticed within the 30-second interval and ran `launchctl kickstart -k`. The gateway came back up on a fresh PID in ~6 seconds after the clean exit, with `[gateway] ready` followed by Telegram polling and `/health` returning 200.

Maintainers who do not run `KeepAlive: { SuccessfulExit: false }` will see the launchd-default respawn behavior on the same SIGUSR1 + clean-exit path and do not need the watchdog. Operators who do (because of the isolated-session SIGKILL cascade or a similar concern) get a clean, scoped recovery mechanism that does not require widening `KeepAlive` back to `true`.

target.title: feat(pairing): add configurable pairingMessage text per channel (#41058)
target.body:
## Summary

Adds optional `pairingMessage` config to iMessage and Telegram channel configs, allowing operators to customize or suppress OpenClaw branding in pairing challenge messages sent to unknown senders.

Fixes #41058

## Problem

When `dmPolicy: pairing` is set, every unknown sender receives a hardcoded message exposing:
1. The OpenClaw brand name
2. The channel platform ("Your iMessage sender id:" / "Your Telegram user id:")
3. The full CLI invocation syntax (`openclaw pairing approve imessage CODE`)

No config option exists to override any part of this. The only workaround is patching compiled dist bundles after every update — not sustainable for white-label or privacy-conscious deployments.

## What changed

- `src/pairing/pairing-messages.ts`: Add `PairingMessageConfig` type with optional fields (`header`, `senderIdLabel`, `codeLabel`, `footer`, `showCliHint`). Update `buildPairingReply` to use config when provided.
- `src/config/types.imessage.ts`: Add `pairingMessage?: PairingMessageConfig`
- `src/config/types.telegram.ts`: Add `pairingMessage?: PairingMessageConfig`
- `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts`: Add `PairingMessageConfigSchema`, wire into `IMessageAccountSchemaBase` and `TelegramAccountSchemaBase`
- `src/imessage/monitor/monitor-provider.ts`: Wire `pairingMessage` config into `issuePairingChallenge` via existing `buildReplyText` hook
- `src/telegram/dm-access.ts`: Pass `pairingMessage` through `enforceTelegramDmAccess`, wire into `issuePairingChallenge`
- `src/pairing/pairing-messages.test.ts`: Add tests for all config overrides and backward compat

## Fully backward compatible

All `PairingMessageConfig` fields are optional. Existing deployments with no `pairingMessage` config see zero behavior change.

## Example config

```yaml
channels:
  imessage:
    pairingMessage:
      header: 'Sender verification required.'
      senderIdLabel: 'Your sender ID:'
      footer: 'Share this code with your contact to complete verification.'
      showCliHint: false
```

## Notes

Discord, Signal, Slack, and LINE call sites follow the same pattern and can be extended in a follow-up if the team prefers to land iMessage/Telegram first.

## AI Disclosure
This PR was authored with AI assistance (OpenClaw/Jordan + Neo agents).
Testing: Fully tested — unit tests in `pairing-messages.test.ts` cover all config branches including edge cases; CI passes (types, lint, full test suite on Linux/Windows/macOS).
The authors have reviewed and understand all code changes in this PR.
