target.title: feat(plugin-sdk): onModelDiagnosticEvent for trusted model.* events
target.body:
## Why

Plugins subscribing to diagnostic events via `onDiagnosticEvent` (from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/diagnostic-runtime`) never see the `model.*` lifecycle. The public API filter at [`src/infra/diagnostic-events.ts:879-886`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/src/infra/diagnostic-events.ts#L879-L886):

```ts
export function onDiagnosticEvent(listener: (evt: DiagnosticEventPayload) => void): () => void {
  return onInternalDiagnosticEvent((event, metadata) => {
    if (metadata.trusted || event.type === "log.record") {
      return;
    }
    listener(event);
  });
}
```

drops **every** event whose dispatcher metadata is `trusted: true`. The runtime emits `model.call.started`, `model.call.completed`, `model.call.error`, `model.usage`, and `model.failover` via `emitTrustedDiagnosticEvent`. So the entire model lifecycle is invisible to plugins.

This breaks observability/cost-attribution plugins. A telemetry bridge plugin needs:

- `model.call.completed` (provider/model identity) to populate per-call span attributes
- `model.usage` (token counts, cost) for per-call attribution

Today both subscriptions are dead code against the trusted emit paths. Concretely, the embedded codex/Responses runner emits the full model lifecycle via `emitTrustedDiagnosticEvent` (see `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts`), but no plugin listener ever receives any of it — model traffic on the dashboard side ends up bucketed as "unknown" instead of `gpt-5.5`/etc.

## Approach

Add a focused, opt-in plugin-sdk API `onModelDiagnosticEvent` that exposes exactly the five model lifecycle event types — and only those — to plugins. The general `onDiagnosticEvent` filter is unchanged: it still drops trusted events, so the safety contract of the broad subscription is preserved. Plugins that want model telemetry have to opt in explicitly.

The new helper otherwise mirrors `onDiagnosticEvent`'s contract (cloned-and-frozen payloads, isolated listener failures, unsubscribe disposer).

A module-local allowlist (`PLUGIN_VISIBLE_TRUSTED_MODEL_EVENT_TYPES`) documents which event types are reachable through this API and is the single place to update if more model lifecycle types are added later.

## Files

- `src/infra/diagnostic-events.ts` — add `onModelDiagnosticEvent` + allowlist (+45 lines)
- `src/plugin-sdk/{diagnostic-runtime,index,compat}.ts` — re-export the new function so plugins can `import { onModelDiagnosticEvent } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/diagnostic-runtime"` (or the top-level barrel / compat subpath)
- `src/infra/diagnostic-events.test.ts` — three new tests:
  1. delivers all five `model.*` trusted types and filters non-model traffic
  2. the original `onDiagnosticEvent` still hides model events from plugins (defends the safety contract)
  3. unsubscribe disposer stops further delivery
- `CHANGELOG.md` — Unreleased / Changes entry

Total: 6 files, +158 / −2.

## Tests

- `src/infra/diagnostic-events.test.ts`: 22/22 passing
- Plugin-SDK guardrail tests (`src/plugins/loader-sdk-import-guardrails.test.ts`, `src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-root-alias.test.ts`): 44 tests, all passing

## End-to-end validation

Built a full `dist/` from this branch, swapped it into a deployed gateway, and ran a real codex (`openai-codex/gpt-5.5`) turn from a Telegram-driven session. Bridge plugin logs show **both** subscriptions on startup:

```
[agentweave-bridge] subscribed to diagnostic events via plugin-sdk
[agentweave-bridge] subscribed to model.* trusted events via plugin-sdk
```

…and during the turn the bridge now receives the `model.usage` event for codex/gpt-5.5 (previously dead-code path):

```
[agentweave-bridge] event: model.usage sessionKey: agent:main:main source: undefined
```

The downstream dashboard bucket that had been showing "unknown" for every codex turn now correctly attributes to `gpt-5.5`.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Plugins subscribing via the public `onDiagnosticEvent` API never observe trusted `model.*` lifecycle events (`model.call.started/completed/error`, `model.usage`, `model.failover`) because the existing filter at `src/infra/diagnostic-events.ts:879` drops every event whose dispatcher metadata is `trusted: true`. On my deployed install, this surfaced as Codex `gpt-5.5` turns bucketing as "unknown" in a downstream observability bridge plugin (agentweave-bridge) — the per-turn span never received `prov.llm.{provider,model}` because the bridge's `model.usage` / `model.call.completed` handlers were dead code against the trusted emit paths.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw v2026.5.10-beta.1 gateway running under `systemctl --user` on Linux (Debian 12, Node 24.13 via nvm) on my NAS (192.168.1.70), driving a Codex subscription session via Telegram channel and the embedded pi-runner. Local install built from this PR head: `dist/` produced by `pnpm build` from commit 52f87ce58a, swapped into `/home/Arnab/clawd/projects/openclaw/dist/`, gateway restarted. Bridge plugin installed under `~/.openclaw/user-plugins/agentweave-bridge/`, rebuilt against the new SDK export.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```
  # 1. Build the patched openclaw from this branch
  cd /home/Arnab/dev/openclaw-model-usage
  CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
  CI=true pnpm build

  # 2. Install over the canonical openclaw dist/
  systemctl --user stop openclaw-gateway
  mv /home/Arnab/clawd/projects/openclaw/dist /home/Arnab/clawd/projects/openclaw/dist.bak
  cp -r dist /home/Arnab/clawd/projects/openclaw/dist
  systemctl --user start openclaw-gateway

  # 3. Confirm trust-gated body in the built artifact
  grep -A2 "function onModelDiagnosticEvent" \
    /home/Arnab/clawd/projects/openclaw/dist/diagnostic-events-*.js

  # 4. Drive a real Codex turn through Telegram and observe the bridge plugin logs
  journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway -f | grep agentweave-bridge
  ```
- Evidence after fix:
  - Built-artifact contains the trust-gated callback:
    ```
    function onModelDiagnosticEvent(listener) {
      return onInternalDiagnosticEvent((event, metadata) => {
        if (!metadata.trusted) return;
    ```
  - Gateway startup logs from `journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway` show the plugin subscribing to BOTH streams (previously only one):
    ```
    [agentweave-bridge] OTel SDK started, exporting to: http://192.168.1.70:30418/v1/traces
    [agentweave-bridge] subscribed to diagnostic events via plugin-sdk
    [agentweave-bridge] subscribed to model.* trusted events via plugin-sdk
    ```
  - During a real Telegram-initiated Codex turn (openai-codex/gpt-5.5), the bridge plugin now receives a trusted `model.usage` event that was previously invisible to it:
    ```
    [agentweave-bridge] started root span for main session: agent:main:main agent: nix-v1
    [agentweave-bridge] event: model.usage sessionKey: agent:main:main source: undefined
    [agentweave-bridge] ended root span for session: agent:main:main
    ```
- Observed result after fix: The downstream observability dashboard that had been bucketing every Codex turn as "unknown" model now correctly attributes them to `gpt-5.5`, with cost figures flowing through the bridge's pricing fallback. No behavior change for any other plugin: the broad `onDiagnosticEvent` subscription still filters every trusted event as before (defended by the regression test `onDiagnosticEvent still hides model.* trusted events from plugins`). A separate regression test confirms forged untrusted `model.*` events from `emitDiagnosticEvent` do NOT reach the new opt-in subscription.
- What was not tested: I did not exercise this against an isolated subagent (`agent:isolated:*`) session, only `agent:main:main`. Did not run on macOS or Windows; tested under Linux Node 24.13 only. Did not run the live `pnpm test` matrix exhaustively — the four review-required test files all pass locally (`src/infra/diagnostic-events.test.ts` 23/23, `src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts` 5/5, `src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-root-alias.test.ts` 13/13, `src/plugins/loader-sdk-import-guardrails.test.ts` 8/8) plus `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` and `pnpm build` are clean.

target.title: fix(tools): propagate external content provenance to policy hooks
target.body:
Summary:
- Adds structured external-content provenance to tool-call policy hook types.
- Propagates provenance through HookContext, before_tool_call plugin events, trusted tool policy events, and tool hook context.
- Detects EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT markers from the active embedded run prompt/session context.
- Refreshes provenance after same-run tool results, so fetch/browser/file external content is visible to later policy hooks in the same run when the wrapped tools share the same hook context.
- Documents the public hook field and source semantics.
- Refreshes the plugin SDK type barrel so extension package-boundary cache keys invalidate when hook contract shapes change.

Why:
- External content is already wrapped as untrusted prompt text.
- Before this change, the post-model tool-call policy boundary did not receive structured provenance showing whether external content was present.
- Policy plugins could see the tool name, params, tool kind/input kind, run/toolCallId, derived paths, and session context, but not whether the tool call followed browser, web_fetch, email, webhook, file-fetch, or other untrusted external context.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Same-run external content provenance reaches the later before_tool_call policy boundary after a web_fetch result introduces wrapped EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT. The checked behavior is web_fetch first, then exec later in the same shared HookContext.

Real environment tested:
Local OpenClaw checkout on branch fix/tool-call-external-provenance at PR head e8800a11833801434e6d48a4a0b3f9b03d06d958, running on Linux with Node v24.15.0 and pnpm 11.2.2. The proof used the real active OpenClaw plugin registry, a real trusted tool policy entry, and the real wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook runtime path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
Ran a live `node --import tsx --eval ...` harness in the PR worktree. The harness registered a trusted tool policy in the active plugin registry, wrapped a web_fetch tool and a bash/exec tool with wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook, executed web_fetch first, then executed exec in the same shared hook context.

Evidence after fix:
Terminal capture / copied live output from the real local OpenClaw proof run:

```text
[proof] pr=91800
[proof] head_sha=e8800a11833801434e6d48a4a0b3f9b03d06d958
[proof] using real wrapToolWithBeforeToolCallHook runtime path
[proof] trusted policy count=1
[proof] shared HookContext initially has externalContent=null
[proof-policy] tool=web_fetch toolCallId=proof-web-fetch-call event.externalContent=null ctx.externalContent=null
[proof-tool] web_fetch returning wrapped external content
[proof-policy] tool=exec toolCallId=proof-exec-call event.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]} ctx.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]}
[proof-tool] exec would run redacted command
[proof-result] first_call=web_fetch externalContent=null status=PASS
[proof-result] second_call=exec event.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]} ctx.externalContent={"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]} status=PASS
[proof-result] PASS: same-run external web_fetch content reached later before_tool_call policy boundary through wrapped tool execution
```

Observed result after fix:
Before the web_fetch result returned, the policy hook saw no externalContent. After web_fetch returned wrapped external content with Source: Web Fetch, the later exec policy hook received event.externalContent and ctx.externalContent as {"present":true,"sources":["web_fetch"]}. This proves the same-run provenance signal reaches the later policy boundary after external content is introduced.

What was not tested:
This proof does not claim full browser automation coverage or production split-context run-owned state coverage. It specifically proves the same-run web_fetch-to-exec policy signal path exercised by this PR.

## Validation

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts
- pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile
- git diff --check

target.title: Agents: add claude-sdk runtime with native-tool bridge + Phase 3 default flip
target.body:
## Summary

Adds an opt-in `claude-sdk` agent runtime driver backed by `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk`, alongside the existing `embedded` (pi-ai) and `acp` drivers, and flips the default to `claude-sdk` (Phase 3).

This is a re-submission of the original #68135 work, rebased onto current `main` (2420+ upstream commits of drift) with review-feedback fixes included.

- **Runtime dispatch** — `src/agents/runtime-dispatch.ts` + `src/agents/claude-sdk/*` introduces the `runAgent` entry point that picks the active driver from `agents.list[<id>].runtime.type`. Default flips to `claude-sdk`; `embedded` kept explicit for the compat window.
- **Native-tool bridge** — in-process MCP server exposes OpenClaw-native tools (`message`, `sessions.send`, `cron.add`, plugin-contributed) to the SDK via TypeBox→Zod schema conversion (`src/agents/claude-sdk/typebox-to-zod.ts` + `tools-adapter.ts`).
- **Hooks adapter** — translates OpenClaw hook events to SDK equivalents; honors `HookInvocationPolicy.enabled` to skip disabled hooks (parity with embedded runtime).
- **Credential modes** — `credential: "subscription" | "profile"` knob defaults to `subscription` so requests use the user's Claude.ai login rather than a metered API key.
- **Session-mirror** — writes SDK turns to a sidecar file so pi-ai SessionManager's truncation race doesn't clobber our transcript.
- **Tool-deny enforcement** — `tools.deny` + `tools.byProvider.anthropic.deny` now scope SDK built-ins (Bash, Read, Edit, Grep, Glob) via the SDK's `disallowedTools` option (matches how embedded filters native tools).

## Plugin SDK surface

- `runAgent` export from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/agent-runtime` for stable runtime-driver dispatch
- `RunAgentResult` / `RunAgentParams` types exported from the same surface
- Legacy `runEmbeddedPiAgent` export marked `@deprecated`; new plugins should migrate to `runAgent`

## Skill → sub-agent generator

Every `skills/*/SKILL.md` and `.agents/skills/*/SKILL.md` now has a generated Claude Code sub-agent under `.claude/agents/<name>.md`; run `pnpm skills:subagents:gen` to regenerate and `pnpm skills:subagents:check` to gate drift.

## Gates checked locally

- `pnpm install` ✅
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` ✅
- `pnpm lint` ✅ (3 cherry-pick-era lint issues fixed in the last commit)

## Rebase notes

- Cherry-picked the 17 claude-sdk commits from the original #68135 branch onto current `origin/main` in chronological order.
- `src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.ts` — kept upstream's `resolveAgentTimeoutMs` import AND added the claude-sdk commit's `runAgent` import.
- `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts` — kept both `runEmbeddedPiAgent` type import (for `EmbeddedPiRunResult`) and new `runAgent` function import.
- `src/agents/subagent-registry.ts` — kept upstream's `sweepInProgress` reentrancy guard.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts` — took upstream's lazy-loaded pi-embedded pattern; follow-up work can migrate memory flush to `runAgent`.
- `docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md` — kept upstream's doc phrasing; doc refresh can be a follow-up.
- `CHANGELOG.md`, `pnpm-lock.yaml`, `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`, `plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` — kept upstream versions; baseline hashes regenerate on next check run.

## Test plan

- [x] Lint green
- [x] Plugin SDK API baseline check green
- [ ] CI green (awaiting CI run on the PR)
- [ ] Live smoke: `openclaw agent --runtime claude-sdk -p "hello"` produces the expected turn

## Related

- Closes #68135 (this is a re-submission with review feedback included)
- Companion PR: #68936 (PR autofix pipeline) — independent, does not block this

target.title: security: scan markdown skill definitions for injection threats
target.body:
## Summary

The skill scanner (`src/security/skill-scanner.ts`) previously only scanned JavaScript/TypeScript-style source files for threats. This PR extends it to also scan Markdown `.md` files, including `SKILL.md` definitions, for injection and obfuscation patterns.

Markdown-specific rules added:
- `hidden-unicode` detects zero-width characters and text-direction overrides that can hide malicious content from visual review.
- `markdown-data-uri` detects data URIs with executable MIME types such as `text/html` and `application/javascript`.
- `markdown-download-exec` detects `curl`/`wget` download-and-execute pipelines, including common bypass forms like `sudo bash`, `/bin/bash`, `python3`, line continuations, and quoted pipe characters inside arguments.
- `markdown-encoded-payload` detects large base64 blocks inside Markdown.
- `markdown-hex-payload` detects hex-encoded payloads inside Markdown.

Implementation notes:
- Added `MARKDOWN_EXTENSIONS` and `isMarkdown()`.
- Added `.md` to `SCANNABLE_EXTENSIONS`.
- Applies Markdown-specific rules only to Markdown files, while preserving code-specific scanning behavior for code files.
- Uses the raw Markdown source for Markdown source-wide rules so URLs like `https://...` are not accidentally truncated by code-comment stripping.
- Adds focused tests for Markdown findings, Markdown/code rule separation, directory scanning, and summary counts.

Why this matters: skill definitions can include rendered Markdown. Without scanning these files, hidden instructions, obfuscated payloads, or download-and-execute commands in skill Markdown can bypass the existing code scanner.

Supersedes #10705 (auto-closed by stale bot). Rebased cleanly onto current `main`.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/security/skill-scanner.ts src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm check:changed`
- [x] Direct scanner smoke for Markdown download-and-execute bypass forms plus benign Markdown prose and table negative cases

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Markdown skill definitions such as `SKILL.md` are now scanned for hidden Unicode, executable data URIs, download-and-execute pipelines, and encoded payloads that previously fell outside the JavaScript/TypeScript scanner surface.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS at `/Users/alexalaniz/Code/openclaw-43469`, based on current `openclaw/openclaw:main` (`24bd0b212f`), with dependencies installed using `pnpm@10.33.2`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the actual scanner through `node node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs` against Markdown sources containing `curl`/`wget` bypass forms, then ran `pnpm test src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts`, `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/security/skill-scanner.ts src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts`, and `pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: Copied live terminal output from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
markdown scanner smoke passed 7 positives and 2 negatives

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  32 passed (32)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.08s

All matched files use the correct format.

Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
runtime-sidecar-loaders: local runtime sidecar loaders look OK.
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
```

Observed result after fix: The actual parser-based scanner caught 7 Markdown download-and-execute bypass samples in the smoke run (`sudo bash`, `sudo -E bash`, `env FOO=1 bash`, `/bin/bash`, `python3`, a backslash-newline before `| bash`, and a quoted pipe inside a curl header) and did not flag benign curl prose or `| curl | bash |` Markdown table cells, while the targeted scanner test file and changed-file gate completed successfully.

What was not tested: No known gaps.

target.title: #92169: Embedded runtime: don't terminate turn after interstitial message sends
target.body:
## Summary

Remove `terminate: true` from the message-tool-only afterToolCall hook so that the embedded agent runtime does not erroneously end the turn after every successful interstitial/progress message send. The model can now continue planned tool work after sending a visible progress update (e.g. "checking the codebase, one moment") instead of terminating the turn prematurely.

## Root Cause

In `message_tool_only` delivery mode, `installMessageToolOnlyTerminalHook` in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/message-tool-terminal.ts` returned `terminate: true` after every successful implicit-route `message.send`. This caused `shouldTerminateToolBatch` to stop the agent loop immediately, preventing the model from re-engaging after the tool result to perform the promised work.

The agent's `stopReason` was `toolUse` (the model expected tool results and planned to continue), but the hook's `terminate: true` skipped the re-invoke step entirely. A 45-day audit across 3 agents showed the follow-through-failure rate doubled on 2026.6.1: 73.3% of promised turns failed to deliver vs 36.2% pre-6.1.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Remove `terminate: true` from the message-tool-only afterToolCall hook return value while keeping the `onDeliveredSourceReply` delivery tracking callback intact.

**Real environment tested:** Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm), kernel 6.12 x86_64. Standalone Node.js script that simulates the agent loop to demonstrate the before/after behavior difference.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```console
node /tmp/repro-92169.mjs
```

**Evidence:**
```
======================================================================
Issue #92169 — Before vs After Fix Comparison
======================================================================

【BEFORE FIX】: terminate: true after message.send

  Turns executed: 1
  Tools executed: 1
  Tool #1: terminate=true | text="Message sent successfully"

  ✗ BUG: After message.send("checking the codebase..."), turn ended.
  ✗ Model never got to call "read" or deliver the actual answer.


【AFTER FIX】: no terminate after message.send (model continues naturally)

  Turns executed: 4
  Tools executed: 3
  Tool #1: terminate=false | text="Message sent successfully"
  Tool #2: terminate=undefined | text="read result data"
  Tool #3: terminate=false | text="Message sent successfully"

  ✓ Model sent progress message → continue working → delivered final answer
  ✓ All 3 tools executed in sequence as intended
```

**Observed result after fix:** The agent loop continues after a message.send in `message_tool_only` mode. With the fix, the model sees "Message sent successfully" and continues to subsequent tool calls (read, search) for actual work. The model's natural `stopReason` (end_turn/stop) determines when the turn ends, so progress-update messages do not erroneously terminate the agent loop. In the simulation, 3 tools now execute in sequence (message send → read → message send) across 4 turns, with the model naturally stopping at end_turn.

```diff
diff --git a/src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/message-tool-terminal.ts b/src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/message-tool-terminal.ts
index 2deaf33d3b..1384f99b89 100644
--- a/src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/message-tool-terminal.ts
+++ b/src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/message-tool-terminal.ts
@@ -191,7 +191,16 @@ export function shouldTerminateAfterMessageToolOnlySend(params: {
   return true;
 }
 
-/** Installs an after-tool hook that terminates the turn after a qualifying message send. */
+/** Installs an after-tool hook that records delivery but does not terminate the turn.
+ *
+ * Previously the hook returned `terminate: true` after a qualifying message send,
+ * which caused the agent loop to end immediately. This prevented the model from
+ * continuing planned work after sending an interstitial/progress update message
+ * (e.g. "checking the codebase, one moment"). The model's natural stopReason
+ * (end_turn/stop) determines when the turn ends — re-invoking the model after
+ * the tool result lets it continue if it was a progress message, or naturally
+ * end with stopReason: stop if it was the final answer. (#92169)
+ */
 export function installMessageToolOnlyTerminalHook(params: {
   agent: Agent;
   sourceReplyDeliveryMode?: SourceReplyDeliveryMode;
@@ -211,7 +220,6 @@ export function installMessageToolOnlyTerminalHook(params: {
       })
     ) {
       params.onDeliveredSourceReply?.();
-      return { ...hookResult, terminate: true };
     }
     return hookResult;
   };
```

**What was not tested:** Full integration test with actual Anthropic API / Slack channel agent. The change is purely a logic fix in the agent runtime hook — no external dependency changes.

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] `pnpm test -- --run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/message-tool-terminal.test.ts` passes (updated test expectations)
- [ ] Change is minimal and targeted — only the `installMessageToolOnlyTerminalHook` function is modified; the `shouldTerminateAfterMessageToolOnlySend` predicate is kept for delivery detection but no longer used for termination.

---

*AI-assisted: built with Claude Code*

commands.command: re-review — reshaped the mirror surface to **pin-from-here** to address the arbitrary-chat-id / authz concern: a thread opts itself in with `/pin on` and can only mirror the session it is already bound to (no arbitrary-target argument), so a pin never crosses a session boundary and adds zero new authorization surface. the operator `sessions echo` add paths now reject any target that is not a verified session participant (`targetmatchessessionparticipant`). also fixed the native streaming fan-out to use the turn's actual origin (`originatingchannel`) instead of stale `last*`, so a webchat/cross-channel trigger renders natively in the pinned channel rather than falling back to the flat post-hoc mirror
commands.freeform_prompt: — reshaped the mirror surface to **pin-from-here** to address the arbitrary-chat-id / authz concern: a thread opts itself in with `/pin on` and can only mirror the session it is already bound to (no arbitrary-target argument), so a pin never crosses a session boundary and adds zero new authorization surface. The operator `sessions echo` add paths now reject any target that is not a verified session participant (`targetMatchesSessionParticipant`). Also fixed the native streaming fan-out to use the turn's actual origin (`OriginatingChannel`) instead of stale `last*`, so a webchat/cross-channel trigger renders natively in the pinned channel rather than falling back to the flat post-hoc mirror.

target.title: Allow Skill Workshop apply through trusted skill symlinks
target.body:
## Summary
- Keep trusted symlink target configuration in one place with `skills.load.allowSymlinkTargets`.
- Add `skills.workshop.allowSymlinkTargetWrites` as the separate default-false switch for Skill Workshop writes through those already trusted targets.
- Thread active config into CLI, gateway, and agent-tool apply paths.
- Validate both `SKILL.md` and proposal support-file writes/removes through the same symlink write-target resolver.
- Add regression coverage and docs for trusted, read-only trusted, untrusted, and support-file symlink behavior.

## Tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/skills/workshop/store.ts src/skills/workshop/service.ts src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/skills/workshop/store.ts src/skills/workshop/service.ts src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `$HOME/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- Testbox-through-Crabbox current-head proof: provider `blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01ktvesre39fhhqj4c3z42et6a`, Actions run `27351180098`, head `8cd27f37b12175e9103bf9bf73996244caabe6b1`

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Skill Workshop can load/propose against skills under trusted symlink targets from `skills.load.allowSymlinkTargets`, but apply-time writes through those targets remain blocked unless `skills.workshop.allowSymlinkTargetWrites` is explicitly `true`. Untrusted symlink targets and tampered support-file targets remain blocked before writing.
- Real environment tested: Linux Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox, Node `24.13.0`, current PR head `8cd27f37b12175e9103bf9bf73996244caabe6b1`, isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` per scenario. Each scenario used real filesystem directory symlinks and the real `node scripts/run-node.mjs skills workshop ...` CLI entrypoint.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Ran focused regression tests remotely: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts`.
  2. Created an isolated workspace where `workspace/skills` pointed to an external target and no `skills.load.allowSymlinkTargets` was configured; proposed a create with `PROPOSAL.md` plus `references/details.md`; verified apply failed with `untrusted symlink target` and wrote neither file.
  3. Created an isolated workspace where `workspace/skills` pointed to a target listed in `skills.load.allowSymlinkTargets`, with no Workshop write switch; verified `propose-update shared-skill` succeeded, then apply failed with `allowSymlinkTargetWrites` and preserved old `SKILL.md` plus support content.
  4. Created the same trusted symlink setup with `skills.workshop.allowSymlinkTargetWrites: true`; verified apply succeeded and wrote both `SKILL.md` and `references/details.md` into the real trusted target.
  5. Created a trusted `workspace/skills` symlink and an untrusted sibling `workspace/other-skills` symlink; proposed a create with support files, tampered the persisted proposal record so `target.skillDir` pointed through the untrusted sibling while `target.skillFile` stayed under the trusted target, and verified apply failed with `untrusted symlink target` without writing support files or `SKILL.md`.
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture):
```text
CRABBOX_PHASE:tests
REMOTE_HEAD 8cd27f37b12175e9103bf9bf73996244caabe6b1
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 7.82s
src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts: 25 passed
src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts: 23 passed

CRABBOX_PHASE:cli-proof
PROOF no_allowlist_blocked id=no-allowlist-skill-20260611-43d5b0340b
PROOF allowlist_loads_but_write_blocked id=shared-skill-20260611-429d46fa49
PROOF allowlist_write_enabled_applied id=shared-skill-20260611-c76a973654
PROOF tampered_support_target_blocked id=support-escape-20260611-b85d70d5b6

blacksmith run summary sync=delegated command=2m14.007s total=2m19.707s exit=0
{"provider":"blacksmith-testbox","leaseId":"tbx_01ktvesre39fhhqj4c3z42et6a","syncDelegated":true,"exitCode":0}
```
- Observed result after fix: The read trust list alone permits loading/proposal creation but not writes; enabling the Workshop switch permits writes only through already trusted targets; untrusted symlink targets and tampered support-file targets fail before any target file is created or modified.
- What was not tested: Live gateway delivery, Discord delivery, and a live model-driven agent-tool turn were not exercised in this proof run; the shared CLI/service write boundary and config schema/help paths were exercised directly, and PR CI is covering the broader repository gates for the pushed head.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof used isolated temporary OpenClaw state/config so it did not mutate operator or contributor workspaces.

target.title: fix: prevent silent message loss from EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError
target.body:
## Summary

When the OpenAI SDK retried an `ECONNRESET` internally (default `maxRetries=2`), the session write lock was released during the retry window. Incoming steering messages could then modify the transcript without updating the fence, causing a fingerprint mismatch on `reacquireAfterPrompt()`. The resulting `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` was caught by `model-fallback.ts` which aborted the run, and the outer reply pipeline produced no user-visible output — the message was silently lost.

## Root Cause Chain

```
API ECONNRESET
  → OpenAI SDK default maxRetries=2, internal retry
  → session write lock released during retry window
  → incoming message arrives via queueHandle.queueMessage
  → activeSession.steer writes directly (bypassing withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites)
  → transcript changes without updating fence
  → original attempt reacquireAfterPrompt() fingerprint mismatch
  → EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError thrown
  → model-fallback.ts aborts the run
  → outer layer hits generic failure branch
  → assistantTexts/payloads never enter buildReplyPayloads()
  → message silently lost (user sees generic error or nothing)
```

## Fix

Five coordinated changes that address the bug at multiple layers:

### 1. Wrap steering in `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites` (`attempt.ts`)
Steering writes that arrive during prompt lock release now go through `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, keeping fence tracking consistent and preventing the fingerprint mismatch that triggers the takeover error.

### 2. Dedicated error handler for takeover (`agent-runner-execution.ts`, `failover-error.ts`)
Added `isEmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError()` export and a dedicated catch block in `runAgentTurnWithFallback`. Instead of falling into the generic failure branch (silent loss), users now receive a clear message asking them to resend. When a takeover wraps a preserved prompt error (billing, rate-limit, context-overflow), the original error is unwrapped and classified normally — only pure takeover errors return resend guidance.

### 3. Scope SDK retry disable to embedded prompt lock path (`attempt.ts`, `attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Extracted `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` helper that wraps the session's `streamFn` with `{ maxRetries: 0, ...options }` — the `0` is a **default** that goes before the caller options spread, so `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` (surfaced via `options.maxRetries` per the chain in `sdk.ts:360`) takes precedence when explicitly configured. Non-embedded callers retain the SDK default retry behavior (maxRetries=2).

### 4. Forward maxRetries through native OpenAI transport (`openai-transport-stream.ts`)
`buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` previously accepted only `model` and `signal`, dropping the `maxRetries` injected by `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault`. Added `maxRetries` parameter and forwarded it through all three native transport call sites (Responses, Azure Responses, Completions). When `maxRetries` is the only option present, the function now returns an object instead of `undefined` so the SDK receives the override.

### 5. Phase tracking in `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` (`attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Added a `phase` field (`prompt_reacquire`, `cleanup`, `write`, `fence_check`) for better diagnostics when this error does occur. Backward compatible — the phase parameter is optional with existing call sites unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Silent message loss on session takeover during the released prompt-lock window. When the OpenAI SDK retried an `ECONNRESET` internally, an incoming steering write changed the transcript without updating the fence, raising `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`, which `model-fallback.ts` aborted into a generic failure branch so the user saw no actionable output. After this fix the pure-takeover path returns explicit resend guidance, the SDK retry default is scoped to the embedded prompt-lock window (with configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` taking precedence), and `maxRetries` is forwarded through the native OpenAI transport.
- **Real environment tested**: Clean Docker container `node:24-bookworm` (Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64) running the patched checkout at the current head commit `2fc3ecee2d`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Built the patched checkout in the container and ran the affected suites plus a real-setup organic takeover test (real on-disk session file + real local `node:http` model server on an `openai-completions` baseUrl + real transport + real fence) and code-path verification, as captured in the v6 proof log below.
- **Evidence after fix**: Copied live terminal output from the patched container at head `2fc3ecee2d` (full v6 log reproduced in the section below):

```
$ docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/app" -w /app node:24-bookworm bash /proof.sh
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (v6, current head) ===
Environment: Docker container, v24.16.0, aarch64, "Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
Commit: 2fc3ecee2d
--- A: Real-setup organic session-takeover proof (resend guidance) ---
 ✓ surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError 1456ms
AFTER-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
⚠️ Your message was interrupted because new input arrived while the model was retrying a connection error. Please resend your message.
BEFORE-fix user-facing payload text (exact, parent 86150a3e51):
⚠️ Something went wrong while processing your request. Please try again, or use /new to start a fresh session.
=== PROOF COMPLETE @ 2fc3ecee2d ===
```
- **Observed result after fix**: The organic session takeover now surfaces a specific, actionable resend message (the after-fix payload line above) instead of the generic non-actionable failure seen at the parent; the configured provider retry survives through `sdk.ts` to the native SDK request options; the native transport forwards `maxRetries`; and the retry-default installer is idempotent with caller > configured > 0 precedence.
- **What was not tested**: No live multi-user deployment against a real upstream provider with an actual network-level `ECONNRESET` was run. The takeover proof uses a real on-disk session file, a real local HTTP model server, the real transport, and the real fence (organic error), with only the upstream provider socket and the "second user" steering write synthesized; the supplementary transport proof relies on unit/loopback tests in a real Docker container.

### Current-head proof (commit `2fc3ecee2d`, Docker `node:24-bookworm`)

Refreshed at the latest head, which adds the configured-provider-retry precedence. Run in a clean Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`, Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64):

```
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (v6, current head) ===
Environment: Docker container, v24.16.0, aarch64, "Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
Commit: 2fc3ecee2d

--- A: Real-setup organic session-takeover proof (resend guidance) ---
 ✓ auto-reply  agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts (1 test)
   ✓ surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError 1456ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)

--- B: Embedded prompt retry default — idempotence + configured-override precedence ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  51 passed (51)

--- C: Configured provider retry survives through sdk.ts to native SDK options ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)

--- D: Transport forwards maxRetries (native OpenAI) + failover classification ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)    Tests  83 passed (83)
 Test Files  1 passed (1)    Tests  241 passed (241)

--- E: Code-path verification at current head ---
installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault accepts configured default:
1088:  options?: { maxRetries?: number },
1098:  const defaultMaxRetries = options?.maxRetries ?? 0;
Install site passes configured provider retry:
3994:              maxRetries: settingsManager.getProviderRetrySettings().maxRetries,

=== PROOF COMPLETE @ 2fc3ecee2d ===
```

This confirms at the current head: the organic session-takeover path still yields visible resend guidance (A); the retry-default installer is idempotent and applies the configured-override precedence caller > configured > 0 (B); a configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` survives through `sdk.ts` to the native SDK request options (C, the new regression); and the native transport forwards `maxRetries` (D).

### Real session-takeover path (organic error, real transport + real fence)

This proof drives the actual user-visible takeover path end to end and captures the real user-facing reply, rather than relying only on focused unit tests. Committed as `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts`.

**What is real (not hand-built):**
- A real on-disk session transcript file in a temp dir.
- A real local `node:http` server as the model `baseUrl` (`api: openai-completions`) that announces in-flight, then blocks before finishing the SSE body.
- The real `openai-completions` transport doing real HTTP I/O.
- The real embedded prompt-lock controller + fence (`createEmbeddedAttemptSessionLockController`), wired through the real `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` / `installPromptSubmissionLockRelease` helpers — the exact helpers `attempt.ts` uses in production.
- The `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` is thrown **organically** by `assertSessionFileFence("prompt_reacquire")` when a concurrent steering write changes the session-file fingerprint during the released-lock window — it is **not** constructed by hand.
- The real `isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError` classification and the real `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catch branch producing the user-facing payload.

**Mechanism (deterministic Promise handshakes, no sleeps):**
1. A real temp session file is created with one session line.
2. On the first model request the mock server resolves a `firstRequestReceived` deferred (the model call is in-flight), then blocks on a `releaseServer` deferred before writing the body.
3. `installPromptSubmissionLockRelease` calls `releaseForPrompt()` at the start of the model call (opening the takeover window) and drains the real transport stream, so the released-lock window spans the real HTTP block.
4. While blocked, the test performs a concurrent "second user" steering write to the **same** real session file, changing its fingerprint.
5. `releaseServer` resolves; the server finishes the SSE body; the wrapped `finally{}` calls `reacquireAfterPrompt()` → `assertSessionFileFence("prompt_reacquire")`.
6. The fingerprint mismatch trips the fence → `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` thrown organically → real classification → real `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catch.

**Before / after (run at this PR's parent `86150a3e51` in a separate worktree; the session-lock + fence code is byte-identical between parent and HEAD, so the organic error reproduces identically — the only difference is the runner's new catch branch):**

```
AFTER fix (HEAD) — vitest verbose
 RUN  v4.1.7
 ✓ |auto-reply| agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts > surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError 487ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
AFTER-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
⚠️ Your message was interrupted because new input arrived while the model was retrying a connection error. Please resend your message.

BEFORE fix (parent 86150a3e51, separate worktree) — vitest verbose
 RUN  v4.1.7
 ✓ |auto-reply| agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts > BEFORE FIX: organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError yields no resend guidance 483ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
BEFORE-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
⚠️ Something went wrong while processing your request. Please try again, or use /new to start a fresh session.
```

**What this proves:** an organic session takeover during a real released-lock model call produces, after this fix, a specific actionable resend message instead of the generic non-actionable failure the user saw before. Honest note: at this PR's immediate parent the before-fix payload is the **generic** failure text shown above (not literal empty output); the originally reported "silent message loss" is the production symptom of that non-actionable failure path. The change at this seam converts the generic failure into specific resend guidance so the user knows their steering message must be resent.

### Supplementary: transport + retry-suppression proof (Docker `node:24-bookworm`)

- **Behavior addressed**: Silent message loss when SDK retries race with session takeover → visible resend guidance for pure takeover, correct error classification for wrapped prompt errors, provider retry settings respected, maxRetries forwarded through native OpenAI transport
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`, Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64) running patched checkout at commit `80bbe803e2`
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` only accepts `model` and `signal` — it drops `maxRetries`. Embedded prompt lock window injects `maxRetries: 0` but the native OpenAI transport never forwards it to the SDK, so SDK retries (default `maxRetries=2`) still occur during the released lock window, widening the race. Additionally, `queueMessage` steering writes bypass `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, causing fingerprint mismatch → `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` → generic failure branch → silent message loss.
- **Exact steps and commands run after this patch**:

```
$ docker run --rm -e CI=true -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app node:24-bookworm bash /proof.sh
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (v5) ===
Environment: Docker container, v24.16.0, aarch64, "Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
Commit: 80bbe803e2

--- Installing dependencies (pnpm) ---
install exit: 0
Done in 47s using pnpm v11.2.2

--- Test 1: Native OpenAI transport forwards maxRetries ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  241 passed (241)

--- Test 2: Embedded prompt lock defaults + override ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  48 passed (48)

--- Test 3: Takeover error handling + unwrapping (agent-runner-execution) ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  184 passed (184)

--- Test 4: failover-error classification ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  83 passed (83)

--- Test 5: SDK transport paths (openai-completions + openai-responses-shared) ---
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
 Tests  32 passed (32)

--- Test 6: Code path verification ---
buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions accepts+forwards maxRetries:
1843:  maxRetries?: number,
1844:): { signal?: AbortSignal; timeout?: number; maxRetries?: number } | undefined {
1846:  if (timeout === undefined && !signal && maxRetries === undefined) {
1852:    ...(maxRetries !== undefined ? { maxRetries } : {}),
1940:          options?.maxRetries,
2380:          options?.maxRetries,
2614:          buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions(model, options?.signal, options?.maxRetries),

Embedded retry default (maxRetries:0 BEFORE options spread):
1083:    return innerStreamFn(model, context, { maxRetries: 0, ...requestOptions });
```

- **Evidence after fix**: All 588 tests pass across the 6 affected test suites in a clean Docker environment.
  - **Transport retry suppression proven end-to-end**: A loopback regression test in `openai-transport-stream.test.ts` starts a mock server returning retryable HTTP 500 errors, passes `maxRetries: 0` through the full native completions transport path, and asserts exactly 1 request is received — confirming the SDK does not retry when `maxRetries: 0` is forwarded through the native transport.
  - Default SDK retry behavior preserved for non-embedded callers (no `maxRetries` in default request options).
  - Embedded prompt lock window correctly injects `maxRetries: 0` as a default that configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` can override.
  - Native OpenAI transport (`buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions`) correctly forwards `maxRetries` to all 3 SDK call sites.
  - Pure takeover errors produce visible resend guidance. Wrapped prompt errors are unwrapped and classified normally.
- **What was not tested**: No live multi-user deployment against a real upstream provider with an actual network-level ECONNRESET was run. The takeover path proof uses a real on-disk session file, a real local HTTP model server, the real transport, and the real fence (the error is organic), with only the upstream provider socket and the "second user" steering write synthesized; the supplementary transport proof relies on unit/loopback tests in a real Docker container.

## Tests

All affected test suites pass (Docker `node:24-bookworm`, aarch64):

| Test File | Tests |
|-----------|-------|
| `agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts` (real-setup takeover proof) | 1 |
| `openai-transport-stream.test.ts` | 241 |
| `attempt.session-lock.test.ts` | 48 |
| `agent-runner-execution.test.ts` | 184 |
| `failover-error.test.ts` | 83 |
| `openai-completions.test.ts` + `openai-responses-shared.test.ts` | 32 |

New test coverage includes:
- Real-setup organic session-takeover proof: real session file + real HTTP model server + real transport + real fence producing `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`, asserting the after-fix resend guidance (and its absence at the parent)
- `buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` forwards `maxRetries` when provided, omits when undefined
- `maxRetries`-only call returns an object (not `undefined`)
- Loopback end-to-end test: mock 500 server + `maxRetries: 0` → exactly 1 request (no SDK retry)
- `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` default behavior (no caller options → maxRetries:0)
- Explicit `maxRetries` override preserved over embedded default
- Takeover detection during owned steering writes
- User-facing resend message for pure session takeover
- Preserved prompt error unwrapping from cleanup takeover
- Backward compatibility for `assertSessionFileFence` without phase parameter

## Changed Files

- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts`
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts`
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`
- `src/agents/failover-error.ts`
- `src/agents/failover-error.test.ts`
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`
- `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts`
- `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts`
- `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.ts`
- `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts`

Fixes #87180

target.title: fix(wizard): report keyless web_search providers as ready, not missing a key
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

During onboarding, selecting a **keyless** web_search provider (one with `requiresCredential: false`) wrongly shows a missing-API-key warning:

> Provider Parallel Search (Free) is selected but no API key was found. web_search will not work until a key is added. … Get your key at: https://parallel.ai

That's incorrect — keyless providers need no key by design. The onboarding finalize summary (`src/wizard/setup.finalize.ts`) decided the web_search message from credential **presence** alone and never checked `requiresCredential`, so a selected keyless provider with no key fell into the "no API key / won't work / get your key" branch.

Why does this matter now?

It's a pre-existing bug, but it's now hit on the **common path**: since #90849 made **Parallel Search (Free)** the pre-selected zero-config default, a fresh no-key install lands on it and is told — wrongly — that web search won't work without a key, directly contradicting the "free, no key, works out of the box" premise.

What is the intended outcome?

Keyless providers report **ready** instead of demanding a key. The fix is generic (keyed on `requiresCredential === false`), so it covers all three keyless providers — **Parallel Search (Free)**, **DuckDuckGo**, **Ollama** — with no provider-specific hardcoding.

What is intentionally out of scope?

No resolver/default changes; no behavior change for credential-requiring providers (their missing-key warning is preserved, now guarded to `requiresCredential !== false`).

## Linked context

Related #90849 (made `parallel-free` the pre-selected default, which surfaced this).

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No — external contribution.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: onboarding no longer tells a keyless web_search provider it needs an API key; it reports the provider as ready.
- Real environment tested: macOS; isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME` with no search keys in env.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: run onboarding with no search credentials and inspect the Web search step/summary:

```bash
env -u PARALLEL_API_KEY -u BRAVE_API_KEY -u EXA_API_KEY -u TAVILY_API_KEY \
    -u GEMINI_API_KEY -u OPENROUTER_API_KEY -u XAI_API_KEY \
    OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/oc-keyless-proof \
    pnpm openclaw onboard --flow quickstart --accept-risk --auth-choice skip \
      --skip-channels --skip-skills --skip-daemon --skip-ui --skip-health
```

- Evidence after fix: live onboarding confirms `Parallel Search (Free)` is the pre-selected `key-free` default:

```
● Parallel Search (Free) (Free web search via Parallel's hosted Search MCP — no API key required · key-free)
```

  And the finalize summary now renders **"Web search is ready — this provider works with no API key."** instead of the missing-key warning. The exact rendered copy and the *absence* of the warning are pinned by a regression test that exercises the real `finalizeSetupWizard` render with a keyless provider (`src/wizard/setup.finalize.test.ts`). (Before/after onboarding screenshots to follow.)

- Observed result after fix: keyless provider → "ready, no API key" note; credential-requiring provider with no key → unchanged missing-key warning.
- What was not tested: cross-OS beyond macOS.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the interactive picker can't be fully driven non-interactively; the finalize copy is verified via the unit test against the real render.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

`pnpm tsgo:core`; `oxfmt`/`oxlint` on touched files; `pnpm test` on `src/wizard/setup.finalize` and `src/wizard/i18n/index` (locale-parity). `codex review --base origin/main`: no actionable findings.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added `setup.finalize.test.ts` case: a keyless provider (`requiresCredential: false`) selected with no key renders the key-free "ready" note and does **not** render "no API key was found" / "will not work until a key is added".

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes — onboarding copy for keyless providers (corrected). No change for credential-requiring providers.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No.

Did security, auth, secrets, or network behavior change? No — display/copy only.

What is the highest-risk area? Branch ordering in the finalize note. Mitigated by the regression test plus the preserved keyed-provider behavior (warning guarded to `requiresCredential !== false`).

## Current review state

What is the next action? Add before/after onboarding screenshots, then mark ready.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? Pre-submission `codex review`: clean.

target.title: fix(gemini): bridge OAuth profiles into CLI runtime
target.body:
## Summary

- opt `google-gemini-cli` into explicit OpenClaw auth-profile forwarding
- refresh/load the selected OAuth profile before CLI backend preparation
- materialize selected Gemini OAuth credentials into an isolated `GEMINI_CLI_HOME/.gemini/oauth_creds.json` for the `gemini` subprocess
- keep Gemini CLI auth epochs profile-owned when the bridge is active

Fixes #88742.

## Proof

HEAD: `4ba89fe8b18f7eef6802752c80bfe8f1359f2b2e`

Validation log: `/tmp/openclaw-pr88742-validation.log`

Commands:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts --reporter=dot
pnpm -s exec oxfmt --check extensions/google/cli-backend.ts extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/plugin-sdk/cli-backend.ts src/plugins/cli-backend.types.ts src/plugins/types.ts
pnpm -s exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/plugin-sdk/cli-backend.ts src/plugins/cli-backend.types.ts src/plugins/types.ts extensions/google/cli-backend.ts extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Relevant output:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  59 passed (59)
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  3 passed (3)
All matched files use the correct format.
```

Source-level behavior proof: `/tmp/openclaw-pr88742-proof.log`

- `extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts` proves selected OAuth credentials produce an isolated Gemini CLI home and writes `.gemini/oauth_creds.json` without logging token material.
- `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts` proves canonical `google/*` models routed through `google-gemini-cli` select the `google-gemini-cli:*` auth profile before `runCliAgent`.

Not tested:

- Full `pnpm -s exec tsgo --noEmit` is blocked on current HEAD by pre-existing errors outside this patch:

```text
src/config/io.ts(192,7): error TS2527: The inferred type of 'configWritePostCommitRollback' references an inaccessible 'unique symbol' type. A type annotation is necessary.
src/secrets/config-io.ts(8,17): error TS4058: Return type of exported function has or is using name 'configWritePostCommitRollback' from external module ".../src/config/io" but cannot be named.
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `google-gemini-cli` backend preparation receives a selected OpenClaw `google-gemini-cli:*` OAuth profile, materializes that OAuth profile into an isolated `GEMINI_CLI_HOME/.gemini/oauth_creds.json`, and passes that isolated home to the real `gemini` subprocess instead of relying on ambient `~/.gemini` state.
- **Real environment tested**: Local Linux workstation, PR branch `fix/88742-gemini-cli-oauth-bridge`, head `ada77084fb0bce5ccb88ba88b38ed803b5b9f0c6`, OpenClaw `2026.5.31 (7150977)`, `@google/gemini-cli` binary available as `/usr/local/bin/gemini`, real Gemini CLI version `0.40.1`. Token values and account identifiers were not printed.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```text
cd /home/rev/projects/openclaw-prs/pr-88742-gemini-cli-oauth-bridge
pnpm install
pnpm build
confirmed backend registration path:
  rg -n 'buildGoogleGeminiCliBackend|prepareExecution|CliBackendPlugin|cliBackends' src packages extensions/google | head -120
  nl -ba extensions/google/cli-backend.ts | sed -n '1,155p'
created direct backend proof harness:
  .tmp-pr88748-gemini-backend-proof.mts
ran:
  BAD_HOME=/tmp/tmp.6jUzYBOeWy PROFILE='google-gemini-cli:jason.allen.oneal@gmail.com' pnpm exec tsx ./.tmp-pr88748-gemini-backend-proof.mts 2>&1 | tee /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.log
sanitized:
  sed -E -e 's/ya29\.[A-Za-z0-9._-]+/<redacted-access-token>/g' -e 's#1//[A-Za-z0-9._-]+#<redacted-refresh-token>#g' -e 's/eyJ[A-Za-z0-9._-]+/<redacted-jwt>/g' -e 's/AIza[[:alnum:]_-]+/<redacted-google-api-key>/g' /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.log > /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.redacted.log
verified no obvious token material remained:
  grep -E 'ya29\.|1//|eyJ|AIza' /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.redacted.log
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
=== PR 88748 direct Gemini CLI backend proof ===
backend_id=google-gemini-cli
profile=google-gemini-cli:jason.allen.oneal@gmail.com
bad_parent_GEMINI_CLI_HOME=/tmp/tmp.6jUzYBOeWy
prepared_GEMINI_CLI_HOME=/tmp/openclaw/gemini-cli-home-m4iizB
prepared_home_is_bad_parent=false
home_mode=700
gemini_dir_mode=700
oauth_creds_path=/tmp/openclaw/gemini-cli-home-m4iizB/.gemini/oauth_creds.json
oauth_creds_mode=600
oauth_creds_shape={"has_access_token":true,"has_refresh_token":true,"has_expiry_date":true,"token_type":"Bearer","has_id_token":false}
real_gemini_exit=0
real_gemini_stdout=0.40.1
real_gemini_stderr=
cleanup_removed_home=true
```

- **Observed result after fix**: `buildGoogleGeminiCliBackend().prepareExecution(...)` accepted a `google-gemini-cli` OAuth profile, created a private isolated Gemini CLI home, wrote `.gemini/oauth_creds.json` with Bearer OAuth credential shape and restrictive file permissions, returned a `GEMINI_CLI_HOME` different from the deliberately bad parent home, and a real `gemini` subprocess started successfully with that prepared environment. Cleanup removed the isolated home afterward.
- **What was not tested**: The proof intentionally did not print raw Google OAuth access tokens, refresh tokens, ID tokens, or private account data. The subprocess proof used `gemini --version` to prove the real Gemini CLI binary starts under the prepared `GEMINI_CLI_HOME`.

## ClawSweeper follow-up

- Privatized the Gemini credential bridge again: removed `CliBackendAuthProfileCredential`, `authCredential`, `acceptsAuthProfileForwarding`, and `resolveAuthProfileForExecution` from the public Plugin SDK/CLI backend type surface.
- Kept the selected-profile bridge scoped to the bundled `google-gemini-cli` runtime path, where the runner passes the credential through a private extension context only after selecting/refreshing the OpenClaw profile.
- Verified no public references remain:

```text
rg -n "acceptsAuthProfileForwarding|resolveAuthProfileForExecution|CliBackendAuthProfileCredential" src extensions/google docs
# no matches
rg -n "authCredential" src/plugin-sdk src/plugins/cli-backend.types.ts src/plugins/types.ts
# no matches
```

### Authenticated Gemini prompt proof after bridge privacy fix

Redacted log: `/tmp/pr88748-authenticated-gemini-prompt.redacted.log`

Command shape:

```text
BAD_HOME=$(mktemp -d) pnpm -s exec tsx --eval '<source-level backend proof harness>' 2>&1 \
  | sed -E -e 's/ya29\.[A-Za-z0-9._-]+/<redacted-access-token>/g' \
           -e 's#1//[A-Za-z0-9._-]+#<redacted-refresh-token>#g' \
           -e 's/eyJ[A-Za-z0-9._-]+/<redacted-jwt>/g' \
           -e 's/AIza[[:alnum:]_-]+/<redacted-google-api-key>/g' \
  | tee /tmp/pr88748-authenticated-gemini-prompt.redacted.log
```

Evidence:

```text

real_gemini_prompt_stdout_begin
{
  "session_id": "463ff78c-cf21-42b4-b8cc-4172073d97a7",
  "response": "PR88748_AUTH_OK",
  "stats": {
    "models": {
      "gemini-3.1-flash-lite": {
        "api": {
          "totalRequests": 1,
          "totalErrors": 0,
          "totalLatencyMs": 1359
        },
        "tokens": {
          "input": 13771,
```

target.title: fix(agents): handle "Not supported model" error format in isModelNotFoundErrorMessage
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92118

`isModelNotFoundErrorMessage()` in `src/agents/live-model-errors.ts` did not recognize the `"Not supported model <model-id>"` error format returned by some OpenAI-compatible provider gateways (e.g. Xiaomi MiFY and similar Chinese cloud proxies).

This caused the error to fall through to `isSchemaErrorMessage()` and be misclassified as `"schema"`, showing the user a misleading message about tool payload rejection instead of a model configuration issue.

### Change

Added one pattern to `isModelNotFoundErrorMessage()`:

```typescript
if (/not supported model/i.test(msg)) {
  return true;
}
```

The regex is case-insensitive, unambiguous, and has no false-positive risk against other error types.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `isModelNotFoundErrorMessage` returned `false` for errors with message `"Param Incorrect … Not supported model some-model-id"`, causing misclassification as a schema error
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11, Node.js, source-level unit test against `live-model-errors.ts`
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```
  npx vitest run --project agents src/agents/live-model-errors
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```
  ✓ src/agents/live-model-errors.test.ts
    ✓ isModelNotFoundErrorMessage
      ✓ returns true for "Not supported model gpt-4o"
      ✓ returns true for "not supported model some-model-id"
      ✓ existing patterns still match
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** Error correctly classified as `model_not_found`; failover and user-facing messaging behave as expected
- **What was not tested:** Live request against a real provider gateway that returns this exact format

target.title: fix(agents): pause yielded main-session runs
target.body:
## Summary

Extend the subagent yield-pause fix from [6f3b5f86](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/6f3b5f8666f3a3877b62d9019b8842455fdf62e1) to top-level (non-subagent) sessions.

When a main session ends via the `sessions_yield` tool, the runner already emits a lifecycle event with `yielded: true` and `livenessState: "paused"`. The runner's terminal lifecycle metadata, the agent-job snapshot cache, and the agent.wait dedupe entry all propagate those flags generically, so subagent and non-subagent runs benefit equally from those parts of 6f3b5f86.

The gap is in `gateway/session-lifecycle-state.ts`. `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` does not look at the `yielded` flag, so the session entry is persisted with `status: \"done\"` even though the runner has queued a post-yield continuation that drains on the next turn. Dashboards, restart recovery, and follow-up turns then treat the session as finished and race the continuation, producing the in-context overflow and out-of-order events the user reports.

## Why the same fix needs to apply to the top-level session

The subagent registry tracks active subagent runs in memory and uses `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` to keep yielded entries in a paused state instead of marking them complete. The main session has no equivalent in-memory registry; its lifecycle state is persisted directly through `persistGatewaySessionLifecycleEvent` into the on-disk session entry. The gap shows up in three places:

1. The persisted session entry says `status: \"done\"`, so `sessions_list`, `sessions.changed` broadcasts, and dashboard UI all show the session as finished.
2. `main-session-restart-recovery` is keyed off `entry.abortedLastRun` and `entry.status === \"running\"`. With status=done it leaves the entry alone, but on a clean restart-mid-yield the yielded session can also end up `abortedLastRun=true` (the cleaned-lock path), at which point recovery would force a `[System] Your previous turn was interrupted...` resume that races the queued continuation.
3. A new user message arrives at a session that is internally paused but externally \"done\" — the gateway dispatches a fresh agent.run, and the queued steered yield-interrupt drains alongside the new user input, producing out-of-order assistant text.

## Shape of the change

- Recognize the lifecycle `yielded` flag in `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` and derive `status: \"paused\"` with `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` instead of `done`. Yielded short-circuits before the `aborted`/`stopReason` branches so partial classifications cannot mask the paused state.
- Clear `pauseReason` on the next lifecycle `start` event so a fresh run reactivates the session, mirroring how `subagent-registry-lifecycle.ts` clears `pauseReason` on completion.
- Add `\"paused\"` to `SessionRunStatus` in both `gateway/session-utils.types.ts` and `agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts`, and add `pauseReason?: \"sessions_yield\"` to `SessionEntry` and `GatewaySessionRow`.
- Surface `pauseReason` on the `sessions.changed` broadcast snapshot and the `loadGatewaySessionRow` builder so consumers (UI, sessions_list tool) see the paused state.
- Tighten `isTerminalSessionStatus` so the type predicate stays accurate after \"paused\" joins the union (paused is not terminal).
- Skip `main-session-restart-recovery` for entries with `pauseReason: \"sessions_yield\"` so they are not re-prompted with the generic interrupted-turn message.

The runner-level changes (yieldDetected -> livenessState=paused/yielded=true) and the gateway agent-job/agent-wait-dedupe propagation that 6f3b5f86 already added are unchanged. This PR plugs the missing seam between those generic signals and the persisted session entry.

## Test coverage

- `src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts`:
  - `marks yielded lifecycle end events as paused with sessions_yield reason` (persisted patch).
  - `treats yielded as paused even when aborted is set` (yield short-circuit).
  - `clears pauseReason when a fresh run starts on a paused session` (start-phase reactivation).
  - Existing terminal-mapping tests updated to include explicit `pauseReason: undefined` on non-yield ends.
- `src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`:
  - `skips sessions paused via sessions_yield instead of forcing a resume`.
  - `does not mark sessions with sessions_yield pauseReason as restart-aborted from cleaned locks`.

## Local validation

- `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` — clean.
- `pnpm test src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts src/config/sessions.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.create-event.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts` — all green (235 tests).
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check`, `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs`, `pnpm format:docs:check`, `pnpm check:changelog-attributions` — all clean on changed files.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI green on \`fix(agents): pause yielded main-session runs\`.
- [ ] Manual smoke: trigger `sessions_yield` from a main-session conversation; confirm the session entry persists `status: paused` and `pauseReason: sessions_yield`, and that a follow-up turn flips back to `running` with `pauseReason: undefined`.
- [ ] Restart-recovery smoke: kill the gateway while a main session is paused via yield; confirm restart-recovery skips it instead of re-prompting with the interrupted-turn message.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Before this PR, when a top-level (non-subagent) embedded `openclaw` run ended via the `sessions_yield` tool, the runner emitted `yielded=true` and `livenessState=paused` but the gateway treated it as a clean completion and wrote `status=done`. The session displayed as finished while awaiting a queued post-yield continuation, racing dashboards, restart-recovery, and follow-up turns. After this PR, the lifecycle persistence derives `status=paused` with `pauseReason=sessions_yield`, restart-recovery skips paused sessions, and `SessionRunStatus` adds `"paused"` so consumers can distinguish a yielded run from a completed one.
- **Real environment tested**: live OpenClaw gateway on rh-bot.lan, pid 65734, build SHA 23804e610c (upgrade-v2026.5.28 with this PR cherry-picked). The deployed `dist/server-chat-*.js` and `dist/main-session-restart-recovery-*.js` chunks carry the new `pauseReason`/`sessions_yield` logic; the proof script imports and executes those exact bundle bytes.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  # On rh-bot.lan, confirm deployed bundles carry the PR logic:
  grep -c 'sessions_yield\|pauseReason' \
    /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js
  # -> 1 (the new shouldSkipMainRecovery branch)

  grep -c 'sessions_yield\|pauseReason' \
    /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js
  # -> non-zero (new deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot branches)

  # Drive the deployed code with three scenarios:
  node /tmp/proof-75662-amittell.mjs
  ```
  Proof script (`/tmp/proof-75662-amittell.mjs`) extracts the deployed
  `deriveGatewaySessionLifecycleSnapshot` from `server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js` via
  `vm.runInContext`, dynamically imports `main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js`
  to grab the exported `markRestartAbortedMainSessions`, seeds a temp
  state-dir with one yielded and one non-yielded `running` entry, and asserts
  three behaviors:
  1. Lifecycle `phase=end` with `yielded=true` -> `status=paused`,
     `pauseReason=sessions_yield`, `abortedLastRun=false`.
  2. Same entry passed to restart-recovery is SKIPPED (no `abortedLastRun`
     write, `updatedAt` unchanged).
  3. Sibling running entry without `pauseReason` is marked
     `abortedLastRun=true` (existing behavior preserved).
  Plus a guard check: lifecycle `phase=error` with `yielded=true` returns
  `status=failed` with NO `pauseReason` leak.
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  === PR #75662 real-behavior proof ===
  host=rh-bot.lan pid=84884 node=v25.8.2
  dist=/Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist

  [1] lifecycle bundle: /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/server-chat-D9hpq-gf.js
  [1a] yielded terminal -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"paused","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false,"pauseReason":"sessions_yield"}
  [1a] OK: paused status carries pauseReason=sessions_yield
  [1b] non-yielded terminal -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"done","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false}
  [1b] OK: non-yielded run preserves status=done with no pauseReason
  [1c] error+yielded -> {"updatedAt":1780285520789,"status":"failed","startedAt":1780285519789,"endedAt":1780285520789,"runtimeMs":1000,"abortedLastRun":false}
  [1c] OK: error phase masks yielded marker, no pauseReason leak

  [2] seeded /var/folders/cn/.../state/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json
  [2] recovery bundle: /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/main-session-restart-recovery-CirHJY3p.js
  [2] exports: i, n, r, t
  [main-session-restart-recovery] marked 1 interrupted main session(s) for restart recovery (pr-75662-proof)
  [2] mark result: {"marked":1,"skipped":1}
  [2] yielded entry after: {"sessionId":"sess-yielded","status":"running","pauseReason":"sessions_yield","startedAt":...,"updatedAt":1780285519789}
  [2] normal entry after:  {"sessionId":"sess-normal","status":"running","startedAt":...,"updatedAt":1780285521266,"abortedLastRun":true}
  [2a] OK: yielded session was skipped (no abortedLastRun, no updatedAt bump)
  [2b] OK: non-yielded running session was marked abortedLastRun=true

  === ALL PROOFS PASSED ===
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: All three scenarios pass on the deployed bundle. A yielded terminal event produces `status=paused`/`pauseReason=sessions_yield` and is skipped by restart recovery (no `abortedLastRun` mutation, no `updatedAt` bump). A non-yielded terminal event still produces `status=done` with no `pauseReason` (existing behavior preserved). An error phase masks the `yielded` flag and reports `status=failed` with no `pauseReason` leak, matching the gating comment in `resolveTerminalStatus`.
- **Targeted tests**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts` -> 22 passed + 15 passed (37/37). Broader: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts` -> 172/172 passed. `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --only=core --threads=4` -> clean.
- **What was not tested**: End-to-end live channel trigger (sending a real Telegram message that drives `sessions_yield` to completion); this is exercised by the existing vitest coverage in `pi-embedded-runner/sessions-yield.orchestration.test.ts` rather than reproduced live on rh-bot.
