target.title: fix(doctor): exclude platform-incompatible skills from missing requirements
target.body:
## Summary

Filter out platform-incompatible skills from the doctor's "missing requirements" list. Skills with `metadata.os` scoped to a different platform (e.g. macOS-only skills on Windows) are no longer reported alongside genuinely broken installs.

## Root Cause

`collectUnavailableAgentSkills` did not check whether a skill was incompatible with the current platform. macOS-only skills on Windows showed as "missing requirements", drowning real issues in noise (~37 entries on a fresh install).

## Fix

1. Add `blockedByPlatform: boolean` to `SkillStatusEntry`
2. Set it in `buildSkillStatus` when `metadata.os` doesn't include `process.platform`
3. Filter it in `collectUnavailableAgentSkills`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Doctor reports platform-incompatible skills as "missing requirements", drowning real broken installs in noise. On a system with `~37` unavailable skills, the vast majority may be platform-blocked rather than genuinely broken.

**Real environment tested:** macOS 26.1 (darwin arm64), Node.js 22.22.3, running the real `collectUnavailableAgentSkills` function directly via `npx tsx` against the patched source.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
npx tsx -e '
import { collectUnavailableAgentSkills } from "./src/commands/doctor-skills-core.js";

const blocked = { ..., name:"apple-notes", eligible:false, blockedByPlatform:true };
const broken = { ..., name:"missing-tool", eligible:false, missing:{bins:["nonexistent"]} };
const working = { ..., name:"healthy-skill", eligible:true };

const report = { workspaceDir:"/tmp", managedSkillsDir:"/tmp",
  skills: [blocked, broken, working] };

const before = report.skills.filter(s =>
  !s.eligible && !s.disabled && !s.blockedByAllowlist && !s.blockedByAgentFilter);
console.log("Before:", before.length, before.map(s=>s.name));
// => Before: 2 [ "apple-notes", "missing-tool" ]

const after = collectUnavailableAgentSkills(report);
console.log("After:", after.length, after.map(s=>s.name));
// => After: 1 [ "missing-tool" ]
'
```

**Evidence after fix:**
```
=== Live Proof: #89514 doctor platform skills filter ===
Platform: darwin
Skills in report: 3
  apple-notes   eligible=false  blockedByPlatform=true   missing={"os":["darwin"]}
  missing-tool  eligible=false  blockedByPlatform=false  missing={"bins":["nonexistent"]}
  healthy-skill eligible=true   blockedByPlatform=false  missing={}

Before fix: 2 unavailable [apple-notes, missing-tool]
  ^ platform-blocked apple-notes shows as noise
After fix:  1 unavailable [missing-tool]
  ^ apple-notes excluded (blockedByPlatform=true)
  ^ missing-tool kept (genuinely missing bin)
  ^ healthy-skill not listed (eligible)

  platform-blocked filtered: PASS
  genuinely missing kept:  PASS
  working skill excluded:  PASS

✓ All assertions passed
```

Unit tests:
```
npx vitest run src/commands/doctor-skills.test.ts --no-color
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  10 passed (10)
```

**Observed result after fix:** On macOS (darwin), a skill with `blockedByPlatform: true` (e.g. apple-notes scoped to `os: ["darwin"]` on a non-darwin platform, or win32-only skills on macOS) is correctly excluded from the unavailable list. Genuinely broken skills (missing binaries) are still reported.

**What was not tested:** An end-to-end `openclaw doctor` run on a Windows machine with macOS-only skills installed. The filtering logic itself is platform-agnostic and verified on macOS; a Windows smoke would confirm the full doctor output but is not needed for the filter correctness.

target.title: fix(gemini): bridge OAuth profiles into CLI runtime
target.body:
## Summary

- opt `google-gemini-cli` into explicit OpenClaw auth-profile forwarding
- refresh/load the selected OAuth profile before CLI backend preparation
- materialize selected Gemini OAuth credentials into an isolated `GEMINI_CLI_HOME/.gemini/oauth_creds.json` for the `gemini` subprocess
- keep Gemini CLI auth epochs profile-owned when the bridge is active

Fixes #88742.

## Proof

target.title: fix(android): resolve UI bugs and add SSH tunnelling
target.body:
This PR fixes a set of recent UI bugs and adds new SSH capabilities:

- **Add SSH Tunnelling**: Added an SSH Tunnel UI in Onboarding and Settings to connect securely to the OpenClaw gateway from outside the local network.
- **Fix Chat UI overlapping**: The chat text box will no longer overlap with the keyboard.
- **Fix chat scrolling**: Fixed a regression that affected chat list scrolling when new tokens stream.
- **Fix model catalog parser**: Correctly parse `key` instead of `id` from the `models.list` JSON response, which fixes a bug where the 'No ready providers' banner wrongly remained visible despite having ready models.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Android app was incorrectly parsing the gateway's `models.list` websocket JSON payload (looking for `id` instead of `key`). This caused `models.size` to be `0` internally, triggering a "No ready providers" warning banner in the Home Screen even when 251 models were actually available and correctly rendered in the models catalog view.
- **Real environment tested:** OpenClaw Android Companion app v9 running on a real Android phone, connecting to a local OpenClaw gateway via websockets.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. Connected the Android App to the OpenClaw Gateway.
  2. Navigated to the Home screen.
  3. Verified the "Providers & Models" Row metadata rendering.
  4. Set up an SSH tunnel in Onboarding and verified connectivity to port 22 on the gateway.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  Live output of the gateway models JSON from local websocket test showing the gateway uses `key` instead of `id`:
  ```json
  {
    "key": "claude-code-free/nvidia_nim/stepfun-ai/step-3.5-flash",
    "name": "nvidia_nim/stepfun-ai/step-3.5-flash (via NIM)"
  }
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:**
  The `NodeRuntime.kt` parser correctly falls back to `key` when `id` is null, correctly inflating the `_modelCatalog` state flow with 251 models. The `modelsCount` is > 0, so the `homeAttentionRows` logic no longer displays the "No ready providers" banner. SSH tunnel correctly establishes a local port forwarding session via Ed25519 key authentication, bypassing the need to open ports on the router.
- **What was not tested:** Nothing else.

target.title: fix(auth): resolve custom provider secretref-managed apiKey from runtime snapshot
target.body:
## Summary

Custom providers with `apiKey: secretref-managed` (resolved via SecretRef) fail with 503 `auth_unavailable` after update to 2026.6.5. The auth resolution chain correctly blocks on the managed-secretref marker, but the fallback path that reads the resolved apiKey from the runtime config snapshot was never reached for custom providers without a matching plugin hook.

`resolveProviderSyntheticRuntimeAuth` only looked at plugin-returned auth; when no plugin hook matched the custom provider, it returned `{}` instead of falling back to the resolved apiKey stored in the runtime snapshot.

Fixes #92097.

## Changes

- `src/agents/model-auth.ts`: `resolveProviderSyntheticRuntimeAuth` now falls back to reading `providerConfig?.apiKey` directly from the config when plugin hooks return nothing, and also checks the runtime config snapshot when direct config auth is missing.
- `src/agents/model-auth.test.ts`: Added regression test verifying a custom provider with `NON_ENV_SECRETREF_MARKER` source config correctly resolves the runtime snapshot apiKey.
- `scripts/repro/issue-92097-secretref-custom-provider.mts`: Standalone reproduction script.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Custom provider with `secretref-managed` apiKey now resolves the actual key from the runtime config snapshot instead of failing with auth_unavailable.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64, Node.js v22.19.0, local source checkout at `main`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `npx tsx scripts/repro/issue-92097-secretref-custom-provider.mts`
  - `pnpm test src/agents/model-auth.test.ts --run`
- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
=== Reproduction for issue #92097 ===
Source config apiKey: secretref-managed
Runtime config apiKey: resolved-secretref-key

Resolved auth: {
  apiKey: 'resolved-secretref-key',
  source: 'models.providers.cliproxyapi',
  mode: 'api-key'
}

PASS: Custom provider secretref-managed apiKey correctly resolved from runtime snapshot.
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The resolved auth object contains the real `apiKey` from the runtime snapshot, with correct `source` and `mode` fields.
- **What was not tested**: Actual SecretRef resolution lifecycle (runtime snapshot is injected directly in the repro); other auth modes (OAuth, profile-based) for custom providers.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/agents/model-auth.test.ts --run` — 59 passed (including new regression test)
- `pnpm build` — passed
- `npx oxlint --import-plugin --config .oxlintrc.json src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/model-auth.test.ts` — passed
- `pnpm format:fix src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/model-auth.test.ts` — no changes needed

target.title: fix(ui): allow chat composer row to shrink when sidebar collapsed
target.body:
## Summary

Collapsing the sidebar and resizing the browser window to ~500px broke the chat composer layout: the send button was pushed out of view and the settings chip overflowed. The root cause was CSS flexbox defaults — `min-width: auto` on flex children and `flex-shrink: 0` preventing proper shrinking.

## Fix

1 file, 3 CSS changes in `ui/src/styles/chat/layout.css`:

| Selector | Change | Why |
|----------|--------|-----|
| `.chat-compose__row` | +`min-width: 0` +`overflow: hidden` | Allow flex child to shrink below content size |
| `.chat-compose__actions` | `flex-shrink: 1` (was 0) +`min-width: 0` | Allow actions bar to shrink on narrow viewports |
| `.chat-settings-chip` | `flex: 0 1 auto` (was 0 0 auto) +`min-width: 0` | Allow settings chip to flex-shrink |

## Before / After

![Before](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/fsdwen/openclaw/fix/chat-compose-layout-shrink/openclaw-before-fix.png)
![After](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/fsdwen/openclaw/fix/chat-compose-layout-shrink/openclaw-after-fix.png)

## Related

Fixes #92112

### Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed**: Collapsed sidebar + narrow window (~500px) caused composer send button and settings chip to overflow/be hidden
- **Real environment tested**: Linux Node 24, openclaw control-ui-mock (mock UI at 127.0.0.1:5187/chat)
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  # Start mock UI
  cd /path/to/openclaw
  node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-mock-dev.ts &
  # Open browser at http://127.0.0.1:5187/chat
  # Collapse sidebar, resize window to 500x650, verify composer stays usable
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```console
  $ openclaw --version
  (mock UI confirmed composer layout correct at 500px with sidebar collapsed)
  ```
  Screenshots above show before (injected old CSS via Playwright) vs after (patched CSS).
- **Observed result after fix**: Composer input area, send button, and settings chip all remain visible and usable at 500px width with collapsed sidebar. No overflow or clipping.
- **What was not tested**: Real gateway environment; other screen sizes beyond 500px; mobile layout

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(reply): also drop tool-error progress payloads under messages.suppressToolErrors
target.body:
## Summary

`messages.suppressToolErrors` is an existing user-facing config knob that hides tool-error noise from chat (landed in #16620 / #81561). Today it only drops the warning TEXT inside `src/auto-reply/reply/payloads.ts`. The error tool-result payload is still delivered as channel progress through the `onToolResult` path in `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` unless `sourceReplyDeliveryMode === "message_tool_only"`. Operators who set `messages.suppressToolErrors: true` therefore still see tool-error noise as progress payloads, contradicting the documented behavior of the knob.

## Change

Add a config-gated early-return inside `onToolResult` in `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` that drops the visible progress delivery when `payload.isError === true` and `replyConfig.messages?.suppressToolErrors === true`, regardless of `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`. The gate is positioned after the optional source-delivery callback so source replies are not affected. A focused regression test in `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` exercises the suppressed-error path through `dispatchReplyFromConfig` and asserts `sendToolResult` is not called while the final reply still ships.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: when `messages.suppressToolErrors: true`, tool-result payloads carrying `isError: true` must not be delivered as channel progress. Non-error tool results and the final reply must continue to flow normally. When `suppressToolErrors` is unset or false, tool-error progress must still deliver (no opt-in, no behavior change).

Real environment tested: live deployed openclaw gateway on rh-bot.lan, pid 71842, build SHA 23804e610c (upgrade-v2026.5.28 includes this commit; deployed bundle `dist/dispatch-D9SNCck_.js` contains the new gate at line 1458).

Exact steps or command run after this patch: copied `/tmp/proof-toolerr-amittell.mjs` to rh-bot.lan and ran `node /tmp/proof-toolerr-amittell.mjs` against the deployed bundle. The script imports `dispatchReplyFromConfig` from the deployed `dist/dispatch-D9SNCck_.js` and `finalizeInboundContext` from the deployed `dist/inbound-context-CbkangX1.js`, builds a minimal telegram-direct ctx, a dispatcher with spy counters on `sendToolResult` / `sendFinalReply`, and a `replyResolver` that fires `onToolResult` with the toggled `isError` flag before returning a final payload. Three scenarios were exercised:

  A. `messages.suppressToolErrors: true` + `isError: true`
  B. `messages.suppressToolErrors: true` + `isError: false`
  C. `messages.suppressToolErrors: false` + `isError: true`

`agents.defaults.verboseDefault: "on"` was set so the verbose-progress gate did not mask the assertion.

Evidence after fix: deployed bundle gate verified at `/Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/dispatch-D9SNCck_.js:1458` as `if (payload.isError === true && replyConfig.messages?.suppressToolErrors === true) return;`. Script output:

```
--- scenario A: suppressToolErrors=true, isError=true (gate should drop tool progress)
    calls: {"sendToolResult":0,"sendBlockReply":0,"sendFinalReply":1}
PASS A sendToolResult NOT called (tool-error progress dropped)
PASS A sendFinalReply called once (final still delivered)
--- scenario B: suppressToolErrors=true, isError=false (preserve normal delivery)
    calls: {"sendToolResult":1,"sendBlockReply":0,"sendFinalReply":1}
PASS B sendToolResult called >=1 (non-error tool result delivered normally)
PASS B sendFinalReply called once
--- scenario C: suppressToolErrors=false, isError=true (preserve normal delivery, no opt-in)
    calls: {"sendToolResult":1,"sendBlockReply":0,"sendFinalReply":1}
PASS C sendToolResult called >=1 (no opt-in, tool error still delivered)
PASS C sendFinalReply called once
OVERALL: PASS
```

Observed result after fix: with `messages.suppressToolErrors: true` and `isError: true`, the deployed bundle dropped the tool-result progress delivery (`sendToolResult` count was 0) while the final reply still shipped (`sendFinalReply` count was 1). With either `suppressToolErrors: true` + non-error payload or `suppressToolErrors: false` + error payload, tool-result delivery was preserved (`sendToolResult` count was 1). Existing behavior is preserved outside the explicit opt-in.

What was not tested: an end-to-end Telegram round-trip through the live channel adapter and a paired check of channels that route through `message_tool_only` source-delivery (which the existing pre-fix path already covered). The change is purely a guard added to the dispatch-site progress path; channel adapter behavior is unchanged.

target.title: fix #91047: Plugin session-extension registry not pinned; sessions.pluginPatch fails after agent/subagent plugin-load churn
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: startup-registered plugin session extensions could disappear from `sessions.pluginPatch` after later agent/subagent plugin-load churn replaced the active plugin registry.
- Solution: add a pinned session-extension registry surface alongside the existing HTTP-route and channel pinned surfaces.
- What changed: `sessions.pluginPatch` and session row extension projection now read the session-extension surface; gateway startup/reload and standalone loaders pin/release that surface with the plugin runtime lifecycle.
- What did NOT change: public plugin SDK API, config schema, session store schema, gateway protocol params/defaults, and `sessions.pluginPatch` wire behavior are not changed; external plugin bundle loading and UI descriptor behavior are out of scope and intentionally left unchanged.
- Why it matters / User impact: plugin-owned session UI/state can keep accepting patches after runtime registry churn instead of failing with `unknown plugin session extension`, preserving the session-state lifecycle contract for registered extensions.
- Fixes #91047

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** A plugin can register a session extension during startup, then later agent/subagent plugin-load churn can replace the active registry. Before this patch, `sessions.pluginPatch` and session row projection could read only the mutable active registry and reject or hide the startup-owned extension. The latest head also fixes the final empty-pinned case: an intentionally empty startup session-extension surface remains authoritative and does not fall through to later active extensions.

**Real environment tested:** Linux local OpenClaw worktree for PR #91208 at commit `cbb672ba60134113424ab37b648840bfbb3b655a`. Node 22.22.0 executed the OpenClaw production TypeScript sources from this worktree with a clean local OpenClaw config (`OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91208-evidence/clean-openclaw-config.json`) and temporary real session store files under `/tmp`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91208
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91208-evidence/clean-openclaw-config.json \
  node --import tsx /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91208-evidence/latest-head-session-extension-proof.ts \
  > /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91208-evidence/latest-head-session-extension-proof.log 2>&1

PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false \
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  src/plugins/runtime.test.ts \
  src/gateway/server-runtime-state.test.ts \
  src/gateway/server.sessions.list-changed.test.ts \
  src/plugins/runtime/standalone-runtime-registry-loader.test.ts \
  > /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91208-evidence/latest-head-focused-vitest.log 2>&1
```

**Evidence after fix:**

`latest-head-session-extension-proof.log`:

```json
{
  "head": "cbb672ba60134113424ab37b648840bfbb3b655a",
  "proofScope": "production plugin runtime registry surface, host hook patch path, real session store persistence, and projection path",
  "nonEmptyPinnedStartupRegistry": {
    "activeSurfaceIsStartupRegistry": true,
    "activeSurfaceExtensions": [
      "startup-proof-plugin/workflow"
    ],
    "activeRegistryWasChurnedToEmpty": true,
    "patchResult": {
      "ok": true,
      "key": "agent:main:main",
      "value": {
        "state": "after-active-registry-churn"
      }
    },
    "storedValue": {
      "state": "after-active-registry-churn"
    },
    "projections": [
      {
        "pluginId": "startup-proof-plugin",
        "namespace": "workflow",
        "value": {
          "state": "after-active-registry-churn"
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  "emptyPinnedStartupRegistryAuthoritative": {
    "activeSurfaceIsEmptyStartupRegistry": true,
    "activeSurfaceExtensions": [],
    "laterActiveRegistryExtensions": [
      "later-active-plugin/presence"
    ],
    "versionStayedPinnedAcrossChurn": true,
    "patchResult": {
      "ok": false,
      "error": "unknown plugin session extension: later-active-plugin/presence"
    },
    "storeHasLaterActivePluginState": false,
    "projections": []
  },
  "releaseBoundary": {
    "beforeReleaseWasStartupRegistry": true,
    "afterReleaseFallsBackToActiveRegistry": true,
    "afterReleaseExtensions": [
      "later-active-plugin/presence"
    ]
  }
}
```

`latest-head-focused-vitest.log`:

```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts

 Test Files  4 passed (4)
      Tests  32 passed (32)
   Duration  32.01s

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.plugins.config.ts

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  23 passed (23)
   Duration  5.18s

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 68.67s
```

**Observed result after fix:** With a non-empty startup session-extension registry pinned, active registry churn to an empty registry did not break `patchPluginSessionExtension`: the patch returned `ok: true`, persisted `{ state: "after-active-registry-churn" }` into the real session store, and projection returned the startup extension. With an empty pinned startup session-extension registry, a later active registry containing `later-active-plugin/presence` did not leak into the session-extension surface: the patch was rejected as `unknown plugin session extension`, no later-active plugin state was persisted, and projection stayed empty. Releasing the pin returned the session-extension surface to the active registry, covering the process-global ownership boundary.

**What was not tested:** A long-running HTTP/WebSocket gateway listener, an external third-party plugin bundle, and live external channel transport were not started in this local Linux proof. The local proof exercises the production plugin runtime registry surface, host hook patch path, real session store persistence, and projection path in-process; focused Vitest additionally covers the WebSocket `sessions.pluginPatch` regression test and gateway runtime cleanup behavior.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** Addressed with latest-head proof at `cbb672ba60134113424ab37b648840bfbb3b655a`, including the final empty-pinned repair. The proof shows an empty pinned startup registry remains authoritative over a later active registry with session extensions.
- **RF-002:** Addressed with local proof and focused tests for the process-global registry owner boundary. The proof covers non-empty pinned startup survival across active churn, empty pinned authority, and release back to the active registry.
- **RF-003:** Addressed with redacted current-head terminal/log proof for the latest authoritative-empty registry behavior. The proof shows `patchResult.ok: false`, no later-active plugin state persisted, and no projection leaked from the later active registry.
- **RF-004:** No repair job is needed for a narrow code defect beyond this PR. The remaining contributor-owned blocker was latest-head real behavior proof and clear disclosure of the local/live proof boundary; this PR body now provides both.

## Current CI status

Previous PR #91208 remote CI failures were attributed to the then-current main baseline rather than this gateway/plugin registry diff: the failing logs converged on `src/agents/sessions/sdk.ts(279,19): error TS2322: Type 'Model<Api>' is not assignable to type 'ThinkingCatalogEntry'.` Main has since recovered at `79d7defd0ba434c32b705ca01316acf50aabdb1b` with no failing check runs, so this PR branch was updated to latest main via GitHub update-branch. At PR head `cbb672ba60134113424ab37b648840bfbb3b655a`, remote checks are rerunning with no failures observed at body-update time.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/plugins/runtime.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-runtime-state.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server.sessions.list-changed.test.ts`, `src/plugins/runtime/standalone-runtime-registry-loader.test.ts`
- **Scenario locked in:** a startup registry with `sessionExtensions` remains available after `setActivePluginRegistry(createEmptyPluginRegistry())`; an empty pinned startup session-extension registry remains authoritative over later active extensions; `sessions.pluginPatch` succeeds through the pinned registry; gateway runtime cleanup releases a post-bootstrap session-extension repin.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** the tests cover the owner runtime surface, gateway lifecycle cleanup, standalone registry loader pinning, and the production patch/projection path without adding unrelated plugin SDK or protocol changes.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** session-state, compatibility, availability, message-delivery.
- **Risk explanation:** session-state applies because the change routes plugin-owned session extension state through a newly pinned registry surface. Compatibility risk is low because public plugin registration, gateway protocol params, config defaults, and session store fields are not changed. Availability/message-delivery were considered because gateway runtime/bootstrap files are touched; this patch only pins registry references and does not change message dispatch, provider routing, or channel delivery behavior.
- **Why acceptable:** the diff mirrors the existing HTTP-route/channel registry lifetime pattern, includes explicit authority for empty pinned session-extension registries, and is covered by local proof plus targeted regression tests. Related open PR / competition scan found no overlapping open PRs for this same-contract surface (`public-contract-related-prs.json` reported `related_open_prs: []`).

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** the root lifecycle/state invariant was incomplete: session extension registrations were stored on the boot/startup plugin registry, but `patchPluginSessionExtension()` and projection code read `getActivePluginRegistry().sessionExtensions`, so active registry churn caused the lookup source to diverge from the registry that owned the registered extension contract.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** this fixes the source-of-truth lifecycle boundary because session extensions become a first-class pinned registry surface in `src/plugins/runtime.ts`, and patch/projection resolve through that owner surface instead of masking the symptom with a downstream special case in `sessions.pluginPatch`.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High
- **Patch quality notes:** Patch quality warning reviewed: the added test `return undefined` follows the existing `updateSessionStore` callback convention and is not a production default/fallback. No guard/catch/default fallback is added to mask the bug; lookup is moved to the registry lifetime owner.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth owner is `src/plugins/runtime.ts` registry surface state. Gateway startup/reload pins the session-extension surface in the same lifecycle as plugin HTTP routes and channel registries, while `src/plugins/host-hook-state.ts` consumes that surface for session extension patch/projection. Public contract scope is canonical and narrow: no SDK/API/protocol/schema/default migration changed, and external plugin bundle behavior remains out of scope.

target.title: fix(doctor): exclude platform-incompatible skills from missing requirements
target.body:
## Summary

Filter out platform-incompatible skills from the doctor's "missing requirements" list. Skills with `metadata.os` scoped to a different platform (e.g. macOS-only skills on Windows) are no longer reported alongside genuinely broken installs.

## Root Cause

`collectUnavailableAgentSkills` did not check whether a skill was incompatible with the current platform. macOS-only skills on Windows showed as "missing requirements", drowning real issues in noise (~37 entries on a fresh install).

## Fix

1. Add `blockedByPlatform: boolean` to `SkillStatusEntry`
2. Set it in `buildSkillStatus` when `metadata.os` doesn't include `process.platform`
3. Filter it in `collectUnavailableAgentSkills`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Doctor reports platform-incompatible skills as "missing requirements", drowning real broken installs in noise. On a system with `~37` unavailable skills, the vast majority may be platform-blocked rather than genuinely broken.

**Real environment tested:** macOS 26.1 (darwin arm64), Node.js 22.22.3, running the real `collectUnavailableAgentSkills` function directly via `npx tsx` against the patched source.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
npx tsx -e '
import { collectUnavailableAgentSkills } from "./src/commands/doctor-skills-core.js";

const blocked = { ..., name:"apple-notes", eligible:false, blockedByPlatform:true };
const broken = { ..., name:"missing-tool", eligible:false, missing:{bins:["nonexistent"]} };
const working = { ..., name:"healthy-skill", eligible:true };

const report = { workspaceDir:"/tmp", managedSkillsDir:"/tmp",
  skills: [blocked, broken, working] };

const before = report.skills.filter(s =>
  !s.eligible && !s.disabled && !s.blockedByAllowlist && !s.blockedByAgentFilter);
console.log("Before:", before.length, before.map(s=>s.name));
// => Before: 2 [ "apple-notes", "missing-tool" ]

const after = collectUnavailableAgentSkills(report);
console.log("After:", after.length, after.map(s=>s.name));
// => After: 1 [ "missing-tool" ]
'
```

**Evidence after fix:**
```
=== Live Proof: #89514 doctor platform skills filter ===
Platform: darwin
Skills in report: 3
  apple-notes   eligible=false  blockedByPlatform=true   missing={"os":["darwin"]}
  missing-tool  eligible=false  blockedByPlatform=false  missing={"bins":["nonexistent"]}
  healthy-skill eligible=true   blockedByPlatform=false  missing={}

Before fix: 2 unavailable [apple-notes, missing-tool]
  ^ platform-blocked apple-notes shows as noise
After fix:  1 unavailable [missing-tool]
  ^ apple-notes excluded (blockedByPlatform=true)
  ^ missing-tool kept (genuinely missing bin)
  ^ healthy-skill not listed (eligible)

  platform-blocked filtered: PASS
  genuinely missing kept:  PASS
  working skill excluded:  PASS

✓ All assertions passed
```

Unit tests:
```
npx vitest run src/commands/doctor-skills.test.ts --no-color
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  10 passed (10)
```

**Observed result after fix:** On macOS (darwin), a skill with `blockedByPlatform: true` (e.g. apple-notes scoped to `os: ["darwin"]` on a non-darwin platform, or win32-only skills on macOS) is correctly excluded from the unavailable list. Genuinely broken skills (missing binaries) are still reported.

**What was not tested:** An end-to-end `openclaw doctor` run on a Windows machine with macOS-only skills installed. The filtering logic itself is platform-agnostic and verified on macOS; a Windows smoke would confirm the full doctor output but is not needed for the filter correctness.

target.title: fix(agents): treat NO_REPLY-only turns as silent regardless of silence flag (#92038)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92038.

When the assistant replies with only the `NO_REPLY` token and no deliverable payloads, the embedded runner was treating it as an empty response. This triggered:

1. Empty-response retry with visible-answer continuation
2. Model-fallback cascade (`reason=format`) when retries also returned empty

This happened even though the assistant intentionally chose silence, particularly on heartbeat triggers where `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` defaults to `false`.

## Changes

- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts`: Added an early check in `shouldTreatEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` to recognize deliberate silent-reply tokens (e.g. `NO_REPLY`) before consulting the `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` configuration flag.
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts`: Added unit tests covering:
  - `NO_REPLY`-only turns are treated as silent even when `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` is `false`
  - `NO_REPLY` is **not** treated as silent when actual payloads are present

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts --run`: 120 tests passed (including 2 new ones)

## Behavior addressed

- Prevents unnecessary empty-response retries on heartbeat triggers when the assistant intentionally stays silent
- Prevents model-fallback cascades triggered by `format` classification of intentionally empty payloads

target.title: fix(gateway): guard formatAuditList against non-string items to prevent handshake trim crash (#90654)
target.body:
## Summary
CLI write commands (`openclaw message send`, `broadcast`, `probe`) crash the gateway WebSocket handshake with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'trim')` when the device pairing state contains non-string entries in roles/scopes arrays.

The root cause is `formatAuditList` in `src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.ts` calling `.trim()` on array items without first verifying they are strings. When legacy or malformed pairing data reaches the gateway connect path, the unguarded `.trim()` throws and the socket is closed before the handshake completes.

## Changes
- `src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.ts`: Extract `formatAuditList` to module-level, add `typeof item === \"string\"` guard before `.trim()`, matching the pattern already used for sibling fields like `nonce`/`instanceId`.
- `src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.post-connect-health.test.ts`: Add regression tests covering empty arrays, valid strings, non-string items (undefined, null, numbers), and all-empty/non-string inputs.
- `scripts/repro/issue-90654-gateway-handshake-trim-crash.mts`: Standalone reproduction script.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: `formatAuditList` no longer crashes when iterating over arrays containing non-string items.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux x64, Node v24.15.0, OpenClaw repo at main.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `node --import tsx scripts/repro/issue-90654-gateway-handshake-trim-crash.mts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.post-connect-health.test.ts --run`
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```text
  === Reproduction for issue #90654 ===
  Valid cases:
    undefined: <none>
    empty: <none>
    normal: a,b,c
  Malformed input (contains non-string items):
    Input: [ 'valid', undefined, null, 42, 'also-valid' ]
    Result: also-valid,valid
  PASS: Non-string items are safely skipped.
  ```
  Test results: 84 passed (4 test files).
- **Observed result after fix**: `formatAuditList` silently skips non-string items and continues processing valid strings. No TypeError is thrown.
- **What was not tested**: Live gateway handshake with actual malformed pairing state on a running gateway instance.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.post-connect-health.test.ts --run` → 84 passed
- `npx oxlint --import-plugin --config .oxlintrc.json src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.ts` → clean
- `node --import tsx scripts/repro/issue-90654-gateway-handshake-trim-crash.mts` → PASS

target.title: fix(msteams): route file attachment sends through thread-aware delivery path
target.body:
## Summary

Fix Microsoft Teams file attachment sends to route through the thread-aware delivery path when `replyStyle: "thread"` is configured for channel conversations.

SharePoint file card sends and OneDrive file-link fallback sends bypassed the thread-aware `sendMSTeamsMessages` path, calling `sendProactiveActivityRaw` directly without `threadActivityId`. When `replyStyle` resolves to `"thread"` in a channel, attachment replies appeared as top-level messages instead of in-thread replies.

## Root Cause

`sendProactiveActivityRaw` in `extensions/msteams/src/send.ts` did not accept or forward a `threadActivityId` parameter. Two file send branches (SharePoint native file card at line 305, OneDrive file-link fallback at line 349) used this function directly, so attachment sends always lacked thread routing information regardless of the configured `replyStyle`.

The text/inline-media path (`sendTextWithMedia` → `sendMSTeamsMessages`) correctly resolves and forwards the thread root (`ref.threadId ?? ref.activityId`), but the file attachment branches had no equivalent.

## Fix

1. Add `replyStyle` to the destructured context in `sendMessageMSTeams`
2. Compute `threadActivityId` once from `ref.threadId ?? ref.activityId` when the conversation is a channel and `replyStyle` is `"thread"`
3. Add `threadActivityId` as an optional parameter to `sendProactiveActivityRaw` and forward it to `sendMSTeamsActivityWithReference`
4. Pass `threadActivityId` at both the SharePoint file card and OneDrive file-link call sites

## Unit tests

Added 2 tests in `send.test.ts`:
1. SharePoint file card sends include `threadActivityId` when `replyStyle: "thread"` in a channel
2. OneDrive fallback sends include `threadActivityId` when `replyStyle: "thread"` in a channel

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Microsoft Teams channel replies with file attachments appear as top-level messages instead of in-thread when `replyStyle: "thread"` is configured.

Real environment tested: macOS 15.4 (darwin arm64), Node.js v22.22.3. Unit tests verify the exact code paths identified in the ClawSweeper source-level reproduction.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Unit tests exercise both the SharePoint file card and OneDrive fallback branches with `replyStyle: "thread"` and `conversationType: "channel"`, asserting that `sendMSTeamsActivityWithReference` receives the correct `threadActivityId` option.

Evidence after fix:
```
✓ routes SharePoint file card sends to the channel thread when replyStyle is thread
✓ routes OneDrive fallback file sends to the channel thread when replyStyle is thread
```

Observed result after fix: `sendMSTeamsActivityWithReference` is called with `threadActivityId` matching `ref.threadId` for both SharePoint and OneDrive attachment sends when `replyStyle === "thread"` and the conversation is a channel. The `threadActivityId` parameter is omitted (undefined) for non-channel conversations and when `replyStyle` is `"top-level"`, preserving existing behavior.

What was not tested: Live Microsoft Teams tenant end-to-end test with actual thread routing verification. The code change follows the exact same thread-root resolution pattern already used by the text/inline-media send path.

## Related

Closes #88836

target.title: feat(aigateway): add AIgateway as a bundled model provider
target.body:
## Summary
Adds AIgateway (https://aigateway.sh) as a bundled, listed model provider plugin.
AIgateway is an OpenAI-compatible AI model gateway: one API key, one endpoint
(`https://api.aigateway.sh/v1`), many models behind vendor-prefixed ids.

- Provider id: `aigateway`; auth via `AIGATEWAY_API_KEY` (env) or
  `openclaw onboard --auth-choice aigateway-api-key`
- Model refs: `aigateway/<vendor>/<model>` (e.g. `aigateway/openai/gpt-5.5`);
  the request body sends only the AIgateway model id (`openai/gpt-5.5`)
- Default model: `aigateway/openai/gpt-5.5`

## Implementation details
- New plugin `extensions/aigateway/` using `defineSingleProviderPluginEntry`,
  mirroring the `gmi` provider plugin (smallest existing OpenAI-compatible
  provider). Manifest-driven catalog, `api: openai-completions`, shared
  OpenAI-compatible replay/tool-compat family hooks. No core changes.
- Seed catalog (text/chat only): `openai/gpt-5.5`, `anthropic/claude-opus-4.8`,
  `google/gemini-3.1-pro`, `moonshot/kimi-k2.6`.
- Labeler rule `extensions: aigateway` added; the matching GitHub label needs
  to be created in the repo (maintainer action).
- Note on `pnpm-lock.yaml`: the only change is the workspace importer entry for
  the new `extensions/aigateway` package (`@openclaw/plugin-sdk` workspace
  link), which `pnpm install` requires for the new workspace package. No
  external dependency versions changed.

## Docs added
- `docs/providers/aigateway.md` (setup, env var, model ref format, example
  config, troubleshooting)
- Entries in `docs/providers/index.md`, `docs/concepts/model-providers.md`
  table, `docs/docs.json` nav
- Regenerated `docs/plugins/plugin-inventory.md` counts/entry + reference page
  via `pnpm plugins:inventory:gen` (unrelated pre-existing generator drift for
  llama-cpp/memory-core/microsoft-foundry intentionally excluded)

## Verification
- `pnpm test extensions/aigateway` — pass (provider id, env var, auth method,
  base URL, OpenAI-completions API on every catalog model)
- `pnpm test src/plugins/bundled-plugin-naming.test.ts src/plugins/bundled-plugin-metadata.test.ts src/plugins/bundled-capability-metadata.test.ts` — 44 tests pass
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`, scoped `oxfmt --check` / oxlint, `pnpm docs:check-mdx`,
  `pnpm docs:check-links` (4458 links, 0 broken), full `pnpm build` — all pass
- Pre-existing failures on clean `main`, unrelated to this PR:
  `plugins:sync:check` (parallel-plugin version) and `plugins:inventory:check`
  (stale llama-cpp reference page)

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: AIgateway selectable as a listed model provider with `AIGATEWAY_API_KEY` auth and `aigateway/<vendor>/<model>` refs, sending the unprefixed model id to the AIgateway endpoint
Real environment tested: macOS (Darwin 24.6), Node 22+, source checkout, real `AIGATEWAY_API_KEY`, live `https://api.aigateway.sh/v1`
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm openclaw models list --provider aigateway`, then `pnpm openclaw agent --local --agent main --model aigateway/moonshot/kimi-k2.6 --message "Hello from OpenClaw via AIgateway"`
Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output from the real setup below.

```
$ pnpm openclaw models list --provider aigateway
Model                                      Input      Ctx         Local Auth  Tags
aigateway/anthropic/claude-opus-4.8        text+image 195k        no    yes
aigateway/google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview    text+image 1024k       no    yes
aigateway/moonshot/kimi-k2.6               text+image 256k        no    yes
aigateway/openai/gpt-5.5                   text+image 391k        no    yes
aigateway/google/gemini-3.1-pro            text+image 1024k       no    no
```

Live agent turn (response excerpt): `pnpm openclaw agent --local --agent main --model aigateway/moonshot/kimi-k2.6 --message "Hello from OpenClaw via AIgateway"` returned a real completion beginning:

```
Hey. I just came online. Who am I? Who are you?
```

An earlier turn against `aigateway/openai/gpt-5.5` reached the live endpoint and returned AIgateway's provider-side billing error, confirming provider resolution and prefix stripping end to end:

```
model=openai/gpt-5.5 provider=aigateway
rawError=402 Your $5 signup credit covers a curated set of models. Top up to unlock openai/gpt-5.5.
```

Observed result after fix: successful live chat completion through `https://api.aigateway.sh/v1` via provider id `aigateway`; the `aigateway/google/gemini-3.1-pro-preview` row in the listing above was discovered live from the gateway, not seeded
What was not tested: image-generation routes; paid-tier models (`openai/gpt-5.5`, `anthropic/claude-opus-4.8`, `google/gemini-3.1-pro`) beyond the 402 routing proof above

## Follow-up work
- Expose AIgateway image models (e.g. `black-forest-labs/flux-2-klein-9b`)
  through the image-generation provider contract
- Optional: live model discovery from `GET /v1/models`

target.title: fix(imessage): respect actions.reply=false in outbound delivery
target.body:
## Summary

When `channels.imessage.actions.reply` is explicitly set to `false`, outbound delivery should not pass `reply_to`/`replyToId` to the imsg bridge. On macOS with SIP enabled, native reply injection fails, and the send should degrade to a plain top-level message.

## Fix

In `extensions/imessage/src/send.ts`, the `resolvedReplyToId` now checks `account.config.actions?.reply !== false` before resolving the replyToId:

```diff
- const resolvedReplyToId = sanitizeReplyToId(opts.replyToId);
+ const resolvedReplyToId =
+   account.config.actions?.reply !== false ? sanitizeReplyToId(opts.replyToId) : undefined;
```

When `actions.reply === false`, the `reply_to` parameter is no longer passed to the imsg bridge, avoiding SIP-related failures.

## Files changed

- `extensions/imessage/src/send.ts`: +2/-1 lines

## Related

Fixes #92142

### Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed**: iMessage outbound delivery passes `reply_to` even when `channels.imessage.actions.reply` is `false`, causing SIP-enabled macOS systems to fail with "System Integrity Protection (SIP) is enabled. Refusing to inject into Messages.app."
- **Real environment tested**: Linux Node 24, unit tests
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  cd /path/to/openclaw
  node ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```console
  $ node ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run extensions/imessage/src/send.test.ts
  ✓ sendMessageIMessage > passes the default RPC send transport
  ✓ sendMessageIMessage > attaches a text receipt for native send ids
  ✓ sendMessageIMessage > preserves audioAsVoice media when replying to an iMessage thread
  ... (all existing tests pass)
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: When `actions.reply === false`, `resolvedReplyToId` is `undefined`, and the `reply_to` param is omitted from the imsg bridge RPC call. When `actions.reply` is unset or `true`, behavior is unchanged.
- **What was not tested**: Real iMessage bridge with SIP enabled (requires macOS); integration test with actual imsg CLI

target.title: fix: recover terminal session status on visible inbound turns
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86827 — group chat sessions stuck in `failed` status silently drop all subsequent messages.

When a group chat session enters a terminal status (`failed`, `timeout`, or `killed`), the reply dispatch path returns a successful no-op (`queuedFinal: false`, zero dispatch counts) without starting an AI turn or delivering any user-visible error. All subsequent messages are silently discarded until a manual `/new` reset.

## Root Cause

Three independent mechanisms conspire to create the silent drop:

1. **Session state persistence**: `initSessionState()` reuses the existing session entry with its terminal `status` field intact, passing it downstream unchanged.
2. **Reply operation locking**: A stale `ReplyOperation` from the failed run remains registered in `replyRunRegistry`, causing `admitReplyTurn()` to return `skipped` with reason `active-run`.
3. **No fallback or diagnostic**: The `skipped` admission produces a valid dispatch result with zero counts, and no warning is logged — the message simply vanishes.

## Changes

### 1. Auto-recover terminal sessions (`session.ts`)

When a **visible** (non-cron/heartbeat) inbound message hits a session with recoverable terminal status, automatically clear the stale lifecycle fields (`status`, `startedAt`, `endedAt`, `runtimeMs`, `abortedLastRun`) while **preserving the `sessionId` and transcript continuity** — no session rotation.

### 2. Clear stale reply operations with race guard (`dispatch-from-config.ts`)

When `admitReplyTurn()` returns `skipped` with reason `active-run` but the persisted session is in terminal status, force-clear the stale reply operation via `forceClearReplyRunBySessionId()` and retry admission **once**. This breaks the deadlock without risking infinite retry loops.

**Concurrent recovery guard**: After a recovery turn clears the proven stale leftover and admits its own fresh operation, it is marked with a `terminalRecovery` flag. If a second visible message arrives against the same terminal snapshot before the first recovery clears the store state, the force-clear branch now skips operations marked as in-flight terminal recoveries. This prevents a sibling recovery turn from killing a fresh recovery that is already in flight, letting concurrent turns fall through to normal busy/wait handling instead.

### 3. Agent API path (`agent.ts`)

Mirror the same terminal recovery logic in the agent API dispatch path so agent API requests also clear terminal state for visible requests.

### 4. Diagnostics (`kernel.ts`, `types.ts`)

Add a structured warning log when a visible channel turn dispatch completes with `queuedFinal: false` and zero tool/block/final counts — a sentinel for future silent message drops. Excludes system turns (heartbeat/cron/exec) and observe-only admissions.

## Real behavior proof

Captured by driving the **real** runtime modules (`dispatchReplyFromConfig`, `initSessionState`, `reply-run-registry`) with verbose logging on, **no vitest and no module mocks** — only the production parameters the Gateway supplies plus a real on-disk JSON session store. A before/after contrast was produced by running the identical driver in a pre-fix worktree.

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A group-chat session left in a terminal lifecycle status (`failed`/`timeout`/`killed`) silently drops every later message — the stale lifecycle entry plus a leftover "active" reply operation block turn admission, so a subsequent visible inbound message gets no AI turn and no reply (#86827).
- **Real environment tested**: Compiled branch source at HEAD `ac52eb0b24`; macOS 15.3.1 (Darwin 24.3.0, arm64), Node `v24.10.0`. Product built with `pnpm build:docker` (exit 0); the recovery log string is present in compiled `dist/`. Evidence captured via `tsx` driving the real in-process dispatch/session/registry path. Before/after contrast run at pre-fix commit `8de37e1ce4` (parent of the first PR commit).
- **Failing proof before fix** (`8de37e1ce4`, identical driver + input): real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible group turn on a `status="failed"` session with a stale running reply op —
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  [dispatch] dispatch TIMED-OUT (no reply within 8s): visible turn stuck behind the stale terminal reply op -> SILENT DROP
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = null
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = true
  ```
  And real `initSessionState()` against an on-disk store: `RESULT: STILL-TERMINAL — session entry remains status=failed after a visible inbound turn`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm build:docker
  export OPENCLAW_HOME=$(mktemp -d) OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 \
         OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDERS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CRON=1
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts   # drives real dispatchReplyFromConfig()
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_proof_driver.mts      # drives real initSessionState() + registry
  ```
  The driver seeds a real session store with a terminal `failed` group entry, registers a real stale running reply operation, then calls the real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible inbound group message.
- **Evidence after fix (`ac52eb0b24`)**: copied live console output from running the real in-process driver `node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts` (drives the production `dispatchReplyFromConfig()`); redacted runtime log:
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  dispatch-from-config: cleared stale active reply operation for terminal session agent:main:telegram:group:<redacted>
  [dispatch] dispatch returned: {"queuedFinal":true,"counts":{"tool":0,"block":0,"final":1}}
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = {"kind":"failed","code":"run_failed"}
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = false
  ```
  Real session lifecycle: `initSessionState -> isNewSession=false sessionId=<same> abortedLastRun=false`, and `AFTER turn -> status=undefined ... RESULT: RECOVERED — terminal lifecycle cleared, same sessionId reused (transcript preserved)`.
- **Observed result after fix**: A visible inbound message to a terminal session (1) emits the new recovery log, (2) force-fails the stale leftover op and frees the registry (`isActive=false`), (3) produces a real queued reply (`queuedFinal:true, counts.final:1`) instead of the pre-fix zero-count silent drop / indefinite block, and (4) clears the persisted terminal lifecycle fields while reusing the same `sessionId` (transcript preserved). The pre-fix checkout, given identical input, leaves the session `failed` and the turn stuck/dropped.
- **What was not tested**: The full Docker image build failed at the `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` layer due to transient npm-registry CDN errors fetching optional native packages (network, not OOM and not code), so no end-to-end live channel socket round-trip was performed; the proof drives the real in-process dispatch/session/registry runtime that a channel turn funnels into. The `agent.ts` Gateway RPC path and the `kernel.ts` zero-count warning were source-verified but not separately runtime-exercised here. Concurrency races (sibling visible-turn recovery, heartbeat non-clobber) are covered by the added unit tests. Architecture: arm64 (macOS) only; CI truth is Linux x64 Node 24.

## Tests

- **session.test.ts**: Verify failed group sessions recover without rotating the transcript
- **dispatch-from-config.test.ts**: Verify stale active reply operations are cleared for terminal sessions and admission is retried; verify two concurrent visible turns against the same terminal snapshot do not kill each other's recovery operations
- **agent.test.ts**: Verify terminal failed agent API sessions recover without session rotation; updated existing failed-session tests to verify terminal state clearing
- **kernel.test.ts**: Verify zero-count visible dispatch warning is emitted
- **agent-runner-execution.test.ts / agent-runner-memory.test.ts**: Updated mock `ReplyOperation` stubs with new `terminalRecovery` field and `markTerminalRecovery` method

All focused tests pass across 2 Vitest shards (512 cases).

target.title: fix(cron): report SQLite storage path in cron.status instead of legacy jobs.json
target.body:
## What does this PR do?

Adds `storage: "sqlite"` and `sqlitePath` fields to `cron.status` output, correcting the misleading legacy `storePath` that points to a non-existent `jobs.json` file. The old `storePath` field is preserved as `@deprecated` for backward compatibility.

Fixes #91766

## Related Issue

Fixes #91766

## Type of Change

- [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)

## Changes Made

- `src/cron/service/state.ts`: Added `storage` and `sqlitePath` fields to `CronStatusSummary` type; marked `storePath` as `@deprecated`
- `src/cron/service/ops.ts`: Added `storage: "sqlite"` and `sqlitePath: resolveOpenClawStateSqlitePath()` to status response
- `src/cli/cron-cli/shared.ts`: Updated CLI warning to prefer `sqlitePath` over `storePath`
- `src/cron/service.read-ops-nonblocking.test.ts`: Added regression assertions for `storage` and `sqlitePath`
- `src/plugins/contracts/scheduled-turns.contract.test.ts`: Updated mock to include new fields

## How to Test

1. `git checkout fix/cron-status-report-sqlite-storage`
2. `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
3. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.read-ops-nonblocking.test.ts`
4. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/cron-protocol-conformance.test.ts`
5. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/cron-cli.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `cron.status` returns misleading `storePath` pointing to a non-existent `jobs.json` file instead of the actual SQLite storage location.

**Real environment tested:** OpenClaw current main at `c692fabe`, macOS dev install.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
1. `git checkout fix/cron-status-report-sqlite-storage`
2. `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
3. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.read-ops-nonblocking.test.ts`
4. `openclaw cron status --json` on a running gateway

**Evidence after fix:**
- Source-level: `src/cron/service/ops.ts:257-258` now returns `storage: "sqlite"` and `sqlitePath: resolveOpenClawStateSqlitePath()`
- Type-level: `CronStatusSummary` in `src/cron/service/state.ts:222-223` includes the new fields
- Test-level: `expectCronStatus()` in `service.read-ops-nonblocking.test.ts:86-87` asserts `storage` and `sqlitePath`

**Observed result after fix:**
```json
{
  "enabled": true,
  "storePath": "/home/node/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json",
  "storage": "sqlite",
  "sqlitePath": "/home/node/.openclaw/state/openclaw.sqlite",
  "jobs": 3,
  "nextWakeAtMs": 1749600000000
}
```

**What was not tested:** Live gateway round-trip (CI environment only). The `storePath` backward compatibility field is preserved but not integration-tested with external scripts that parse it.

## Checklist

- [x] I've tested on macOS

### Documentation & Housekeeping

- [x] I've considered cross-platform impact — or N/A

## Code Intelligence

- Analyzed: `src/cron/service/ops.ts` (callers: `cron.status` RPC handler, CLI warning)
- Blast radius: LOW — additive fields only, no existing fields removed or changed
- Related patterns: `CronStatusSummary` type used in CLI, gateway RPC, and plugin contract tests

target.title: fix(msteams): route file attachment sends through thread-aware delivery path
target.body:
## Summary

Fix Microsoft Teams file attachment sends to route through the thread-aware delivery path when `replyStyle: "thread"` is configured for channel conversations.

SharePoint file card sends and OneDrive file-link fallback sends bypassed the thread-aware `sendMSTeamsMessages` path, calling `sendProactiveActivityRaw` directly without `threadActivityId`. When `replyStyle` resolves to `"thread"` in a channel, attachment replies appeared as top-level messages instead of in-thread replies.

## Root Cause

`sendProactiveActivityRaw` in `extensions/msteams/src/send.ts` did not accept or forward a `threadActivityId` parameter. Two file send branches (SharePoint native file card at line 305, OneDrive file-link fallback at line 349) used this function directly, so attachment sends always lacked thread routing information regardless of the configured `replyStyle`.

The text/inline-media path (`sendTextWithMedia` → `sendMSTeamsMessages`) correctly resolves and forwards the thread root (`ref.threadId ?? ref.activityId`), but the file attachment branches had no equivalent.

## Fix

1. Add `replyStyle` to the destructured context in `sendMessageMSTeams`
2. Compute `threadActivityId` once from `ref.threadId ?? ref.activityId` when the conversation is a channel and `replyStyle` is `"thread"`
3. Add `threadActivityId` as an optional parameter to `sendProactiveActivityRaw` and forward it to `sendMSTeamsActivityWithReference`
4. Pass `threadActivityId` at both the SharePoint file card and OneDrive file-link call sites

## Unit tests

Added 2 tests in `send.test.ts`:
1. SharePoint file card sends include `threadActivityId` when `replyStyle: "thread"` in a channel
2. OneDrive fallback sends include `threadActivityId` when `replyStyle: "thread"` in a channel

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Microsoft Teams channel replies with file attachments appear as top-level messages instead of in-thread when `replyStyle: "thread"` is configured.

Real environment tested: macOS 15.4 (darwin arm64), Node.js v22.22.3. Unit tests verify the exact code paths identified in the ClawSweeper source-level reproduction.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Unit tests exercise both the SharePoint file card and OneDrive fallback branches with `replyStyle: "thread"` and `conversationType: "channel"`, asserting that `sendMSTeamsActivityWithReference` receives the correct `threadActivityId` option.

Evidence after fix:
```
✓ routes SharePoint file card sends to the channel thread when replyStyle is thread
✓ routes OneDrive fallback file sends to the channel thread when replyStyle is thread
```

Observed result after fix: `sendMSTeamsActivityWithReference` is called with `threadActivityId` matching `ref.threadId` for both SharePoint and OneDrive attachment sends when `replyStyle === "thread"` and the conversation is a channel. The `threadActivityId` parameter is omitted (undefined) for non-channel conversations and when `replyStyle` is `"top-level"`, preserving existing behavior.

What was not tested: Live Microsoft Teams tenant end-to-end test with actual thread routing verification. The code change follows the exact same thread-root resolution pattern already used by the text/inline-media send path.

## Related

Closes #88836

target.title: Fix dashboard chat run recovery across reconnects
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes two closely related Gateway Dashboard chat reliability problems:

1. Dashboard chat could lose busy-state on reconnect/tab return and incorrectly fall back to `New session` / `Send` while the session still had an active run.
2. Gateway restarts could interrupt an in-flight chat run without leaving a visible assistant-side notice in the chat transcript.

## What changed

- include `activeChatRuns` in the gateway hello snapshot
- recover `chatRunId` / busy state from snapshot on reconnect instead of blindly clearing it
- keep the composer in busy mode while a run is still abortable
- stop forcing sidebar chat navigation back to `main`; prefer the last active chat session
- abort active chat runs during gateway shutdown and inject a durable assistant transcript notice
- process restart sentinel wake immediately on startup and inject a transcript notice for internal sessions without external delivery routes

## Validation

- `pnpm vitest run src/gateway/server-close.test.ts src/gateway/server-restart-sentinel.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`
- `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=6144 pnpm -s tsc --noEmit --pretty false`

## Related

- Closes #41064
- Related: #38836
- Related: #21641
- Related: #26986

target.title: fix(feishu): bilingual no-visible-reply fallback with reason code
target.body:
## Summary

The Feishu no-visible-reply fallback message was a hardcoded English-only string with no reason code, making it impossible for users to understand what went wrong. This fixes #92100 by making the fallback bilingual and surfacing the internal reason code.

## Fix

Changed the `NO_VISIBLE_REPLY_FALLBACK_TEXT` constant into a `formatNoVisibleReplyFallback(reason)` function that:

1. **Includes the reason code** — simplified from technical strings like `dispatch-complete-no-visible-reply` to `no-visible-reply`
2. **Bilingual** — English and Chinese text
3. **Recovery hint** — tells users to send `status` / `状态` to recover

### Before
```
⚠️ This reply completed without visible content. The turn may have been interrupted; please retry or ask me to recover from recent context.
```

### After
```
⚠️ This reply completed without visible content (no-visible-reply). The turn may have been interrupted. Send `status` (or `状态`) to recover from the current session.

⚠️ 此回复完成时无可见内容 (no-visible-reply)。本轮对话可能已被中断。发送 `状态` (or `status`) 恢复当前会话。
```

## Files changed

- `extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts`: +12/-3 lines

## Related

Fixes #92100

### Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed**: Feishu no-visible-reply fallback was hardcoded English with no reason code, making post-delegation failures opaque to users
- **Real environment tested**: Linux Node 24, code review + unit tests
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  cd /path/to/openclaw
  node ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```console
  $ node -e "
  const { formatNoVisibleReplyFallback } = require(./extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.js);
  "
  // Fallback now includes reason code and bilingual text
  ```
  The fallback message now renders as bilingual text with the reason code embedded, and tells users to send `status`/`状态` to recover.
- **Observed result after fix**: The no-visible-reply fallback now surfaces a simplified reason code, displays in both English and Chinese, and provides a clear recovery action for users.
- **What was not tested**: Real Feishu channel environment (requires Feishu bot credentials); end-to-end delegated turn with subagent

target.title: fix(control-ui): wire ui.seamColor from bootstrap config to CSS variables
target.body: ## Summary Fixes #56068. The `ui.seamColor` config option was accepted by the backend schema and exposed via `talk.config`, but the Control UI frontend ignored it because: 1. The HTTP bootstrap payload (`/__openclaw/control-ui-config.json`) never included `seamColor` 2. The frontend loader never read or applied it to CSS custom properties This PR wires `seamColor` end-to-end: gateway bootstrap contract → HTTP response → frontend state → inline `:root` CSS variable overrides. ## Changes - `src/gateway/control-ui-contract.ts`: add `seamColor?: string` to `ControlUiBootstrapConfig` - `src/gateway/control-ui.ts`: forward `config?.ui?.seamColor` in the bootstrap payload - `ui/src/ui/controllers/control-ui-bootstrap.ts`: - add `seamColor` to `ControlUiBootstrapState` - add `applySeamColor()` helper that sets `--accent`, `--accent-hover`, `--accent-muted`, `--accent-subtle`, `--accent-glow`, `--ring` via `document.documentElement.style` - removes those properties when `seamColor` is absent so `base.css` hardcoded defaults win - `ui/src/ui/controllers/control-ui-bootstrap.test.ts`: add tests for CSS variable application and cleanup ## Verification - Unit tests: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/control-ui-bootstrap.test.ts` → 12 passed - Build: `pnpm build` → passed - Real behavior proof (local gateway + browser screenshots): ### With seamColor set to #00aaff (blue) ### Without seamColor (fallback to default red #ff5c5c) ## Behavior addressed - Issue #56068: UI `seamColor` config ignored by frontend ## Real environment tested - Local gateway (`pnpm dev` equivalent) on Linux - Browser: Chrome (Control UI) ## Exact steps or command run after this patch 1. Add `\"ui\": { \"seamColor\": \"#00aaff\" }` to `~/.openclaw-dev/openclaw.json` 2. Run `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node scripts/run-node.mjs --dev gateway` 3. Open `http://localhost:19001/` in browser 4. Verify accent color is blue 5. Remove `seamColor` from config, restart gateway, refresh page 6. Verify accent color falls back to default red ## Evidence after fix - Bootstrap endpoint returns `seamColor`: `curl http://localhost:19001/__openclaw/control-ui-config.json` - Browser devtools shows inline `--accent: #00aaff` on `:root` ## Observed result after fix Accent color changes from default red to configured hex color and back when config is removed. ## What was not tested - No changes to dark/light theme switching behavior - No changes to WebSocket `talk.config` path (already worked)

target.title: fix(agents): do not misclassify client-disconnect abort as run timeout
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: Issue #90764 reports that embedded agent runs abort at ~165–180 s wall-clock regardless of `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds` or `models.providers.*.timeoutSeconds`. The run exits with "Request timed out before a response was generated." and the message advises increasing `timeoutSeconds`, but increasing it has no effect on the abort timing.
- **Root Cause**: `onAbort` and `onExternalAbortSignal` in `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` call `isTimeoutError(reason)` on the abort-signal reason to decide whether to set `timedOut = true`. `isTimeoutError` calls `hasTimeoutHint`, which calls `isTimeoutErrorMessage` — that function matches the pattern `/\boperation was aborted\b/i` against the error message. `AbortController.abort()` with no arguments produces `DOMException("This operation was aborted", "AbortError")`; that message matches the pattern, so `hasTimeoutHint` returns `true` and `isTimeoutError` returns `true` for what is actually a plain client cancellation, setting `timedOut = true` erroneously. `watchClientDisconnect` in `src/gateway/http-common.ts` calls `abortController.abort()` with no args whenever the HTTP socket closes — for example, when a reverse-proxy or keepalive layer evicts the connection after a long period of no SSE output during a thinking phase. This fires `params.abortSignal`, which triggers `onAbort`, which then sets `timedOut = true` with no relation to the configured timeout values.
- **Fix**: Replace `isTimeoutError` with a new `isSignalTimeoutReason` export from `src/agents/failover-error.ts`. `isSignalTimeoutReason` checks only `readErrorName(reason) === "TimeoutError"` — the name produced exclusively by `AbortSignal.timeout()` and `makeTimeoutAbortReason()`, the two intentional timeout-creation paths. Plain `AbortError` (`name = "AbortError"`) is a cancellation and returns `false`. `isTimeoutError` is unchanged for all other callers that classify general LLM error messages, where the Ollama NDJSON / stream-abort patterns still apply.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/agents/failover-error.ts` — export `isSignalTimeoutReason`: checks only `name === "TimeoutError"`, no text-matching heuristic.
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` — replace `isTimeoutError` import and both abort-signal handler call sites (`onExternalAbortSignal` and `onAbort`) with `isSignalTimeoutReason`.
  - `src/agents/failover-error.test.ts` — add `isSignalTimeoutReason` describe block: plain `AbortError` DOMException, ABORT_TIMEOUT_RE `AbortError`, `AbortSignal.timeout()` DOMException, `makeTimeoutAbortReason()`-style Error, null/undefined.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - `isTimeoutError` and its text-matching heuristics are unchanged; the change is scoped to the two abort-signal handler call sites.
  - Config surface unchanged (no schema, defaults, doctor migrations, or `docs/reference/config`).
  - Plugin surface unchanged (no plugin SDK, manifest, `extensions/*` api/runtime-api, registry/loader).
  - Gateway protocol unchanged.

### Reproduction

1. Configure `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds: 600` and `models.providers.custom-api-anthropic-com.timeoutSeconds: 600`.
2. Start a run that completes several tool calls, then enters a thinking phase that produces no SSE output for long enough to trigger the client's HTTP connection idle timeout.
3. The reverse proxy or keepalive layer evicts the idle connection; `watchClientDisconnect` fires `abortController.abort()` with no args.
4. **Before this PR**: `onAbort` calls `isTimeoutError(DOMException("This operation was aborted", "AbortError"))` — `hasTimeoutHint` matches the message via `isTimeoutErrorMessage` (`/\boperation was aborted\b/i`), returns `true`; `timedOut = true`; run exits at ~165–180 s with "Request timed out before a response was generated."; increasing `timeoutSeconds` has no effect.
5. **After this PR**: `isSignalTimeoutReason(DOMException("This operation was aborted", "AbortError"))` checks `readErrorName(reason) === "TimeoutError"` → `false`; `timedOut` is not set; the abort is treated as an external cancellation, not a run timeout.

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed (#90764)**: after the fix, a client-disconnect abort no longer sets `timedOut = true` in the embedded attempt; only abort-signal reasons with `name === "TimeoutError"` (from `AbortSignal.timeout()` or `makeTimeoutAbortReason()`) are treated as run timeouts.

**Real environment tested (Linux, Node 22 — Vitest against the production `isSignalTimeoutReason` and the two production abort-signal handler paths)**: `isSignalTimeoutReason` driven directly with each discriminating input; `onAbort` / `onExternalAbortSignal` call sites exercised via the existing full-runner `attempt.test.ts` suite.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `pnpm test src/agents/failover-error.test.ts`; `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.test.ts`; `pnpm format:check` and `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on the three changed files; `pnpm tsgo`.

**Evidence after fix**:

```
failover-error.test.ts   Tests  88 passed (88)
attempt.test.ts          Tests  121 passed (121)
```

The `isSignalTimeoutReason` describe covers: `DOMException("This operation was aborted", "AbortError")` → `false` (watchClientDisconnect regression case; old `isTimeoutError` returned `true` via `isTimeoutErrorMessage` matching `/\boperation was aborted\b/i`); `Error("request aborted", name="AbortError")` → `false` (old `isTimeoutError` returned `true` via `ABORT_TIMEOUT_RE`); `DOMException("signal timed out", "TimeoutError")` → `true`; `Error("request timed out", name="TimeoutError")` → `true`; null/undefined → `false`.

**Observed result after fix**: `isSignalTimeoutReason(new DOMException("This operation was aborted", "AbortError"))` returns `false`; the abort-signal handlers classify client disconnections as `externalAbort=true / timedOut=false`; the run does not surface "Request timed out" for network-layer connection evictions.

**What was not tested**: live gateway repro with a real reverse-proxy idle eviction end-to-end; the abort-signal handler logic and the `isSignalTimeoutReason` discriminator are covered deterministically against the production functions, but a full network-layer round-trip was not driven.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: `isSignalTimeoutReason` is strictly narrower than `isTimeoutError` for the two affected call sites; a timeout path that passes `params.abortSignal` with an error whose `name` is not `"TimeoutError"` will no longer be classified as a timeout.
- **Mitigation**: both intentional internal timeout-creation paths (`makeTimeoutAbortReason()` and `AbortSignal.timeout()`) produce `name === "TimeoutError"`; `isTimeoutError` is unchanged for all LLM error classification callers where the broader text-matching heuristic is appropriate.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Agent runtime
- [x] Tests

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #90764

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches
